description: Adventurers who don't wear armor travel in durable clothing. Though it's not armor and uses your unarmored defense proficiency, it still has a Dex cap and can be etched with runes.

name: Eye Garnets
description: Large red rubies you pried from a statue of Xar-Azmak.

name: Eyecatcher
description: An eyecatcher is a simple tassel, reflective basket hilt, or other accessory attached to a weapon to serve as a distraction. You gain a +1 item bonus to Deception checks to while using a weapon with an eyecatcher attached. An eyecatcher affects the balancing of a weapon, making it slightly more unwieldy. This increases the weapon's Bulk by 1 and grants a –1 penalty to damage rolls with the weapon.

name: Falchion
description: This heavy, curved two-handed sword deals 1d10 slashing damage and has the forceful and sweep traits.

name: Fan of the Four Winds
description: This ornate fan once belonged to a sage of Gozreh. Activate it (two actions, concentrate, manipulate; once per day) to fan in a direction and cast gust of wind; if directed at a ship's sail, the vessel instead gains a +10-foot circumstance bonus to its Speed for 8 hours. As a relic, the fan can awaken further gifts: feather step and wind barrier (minor), lightning storm and propelling winds (major), and living storm (grand).

name: Fear Gem
description: A talisman set with a smoky gem, affixed to a weapon. Activate it (two actions) to make a melee Strike; if you hit and deal damage, the target is frightened 1, or frightened 2 on a critical hit. If you have the Intimidating Strike feat, increase the frightened value by 1.

consumable.note: Strike → frightened 1 (2 on crit)

name: Fearcracker
description: A fearcracker contains fragments of broken mirrors and reagents that pop and smoke when consumed. When thrown down in your space as part of a Mirror Image spell, your and your images' appearance twists nightmarishly. When an image is destroyed, it "dies" in a disturbing fashion, rendering the attacker Frightened 1. This aspect of the spell has the emotion, fear, and mental traits.

catalyst.effect: When one of your images is destroyed, the attacker becomes Frightened 1 (emotion, fear, mental).

name: Featherlight Fletching
description: Activate (one-action, manipulate). Elven archers were the first to create the featherlight fletching, meant for long-range battles, though artisans of many other ancestries have now learned the art of crafting these whetstones. A featherlight fletching is a feather-shaped trinket crafted of spun silver filigree and aids projectiles in flying true, even over great distances. A ranged weapon under the effects of a featherlight fletching doubles its range increment for 1 minute.

whetstone.reminder: Range increment doubled.

name: Clothing (Fine)
description: Fine clothing appropriate for a noble court or formal occasion.

name: Fist Shield
description: This rectangular metal shield is usually about 1 foot square and primarily used by duelists. It offers slightly more protection than the buckler at the expense of requiring the use of a hand to wield it the entire time.

name: Flail
description: This weapon consists of a wooden handle attached to a spiked ball or cylinder by a chain, rope, or strap of leather.

name: Flask of Fellowship
description: This metal drinking flask is 4 inches in diameter and 10 inches tall, with a screw top covered by four nested metal cups. When you share drinks as part of a Make an Impression action, the drink that pours out is exactly what the target would most like to have a dram or two of, and you gain a +1 item bonus to that Diplomacy check. The drinks are purely social and provide no nourishment or intoxication. The GM might prevent its use when sharing a drink would be inappropriate.

name: Flint & Steel
description: A camper's classic for starting fires.

name: Fluid Form Staff (Greater)
description: This flexible staff channels transmutation magic. It holds pest form (1st); animal form, enlarge, shrink (2nd); feet to fins, insect form (3rd); and aerial form, animal form, dinosaur form (4th).

name: Foe-Sensing Rod
description: Activate (one-action, manipulate). Imbued with a spirit fragment that continually surveys the world around it, this crystalline bar is roughened to form a file at one end. When you apply the foe-sensing rod to a weapon, choose aberration, animal, beast, celestial, construct, dragon, elemental, fey, fiend, giant, monitor, ooze, undead, or both fungus and plant. The spirit fragment is transferred into your weapon for 1 hour, keeping watch for creatures with the chosen trait or traits. The affected weapon vibrates slightly if such a creature is within 60 feet of you, unless the creature is disguised or Hidden and has a Deception or Stealth DC of 26 or higher.

whetstone.reminder: The weapon vibrates when a creature with the chosen trait is within 60 feet (unless disguised/Hidden with Deception or Stealth DC 26+).

name: Frenzied Quintessence
description: Activate (one-action, manipulate). Contained within this small iron-bound glass sphere is a raging whirlwind of colors, each fighting for prominence. After a successful Strike with a weapon enhanced by frenzied quintessence, you are Quickened for 1 round. You can use the additional action only to Stride toward a foe or Strike.

onHit.note: You are Quickened for 1 round (extra action: Stride toward a foe, or Strike).

whetstone.reminder: After a successful Strike: Quickened 1 round (Stride toward a foe or Strike).

name: Frost Vial
description: A vial of supercooled liquid that numbs and slows. Deals 1d6 cold and 1 cold splash; on a hit the target takes a −5-foot status penalty to its Speeds until the end of its next turn.

strikes.name: Frost Vial
strikes.description: Deals 1d6 cold and 1 cold splash; on a hit the target takes a −5-foot status penalty to its Speeds until the end of its next turn.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Frost Vial (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1d6 cold, 1 splash; −5 ft Speed on hit

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Frost Vial (Moderate)
variants.effect: 2d6 cold, 2 splash; −5 ft Speed on hit

strikes.name: Frost Vial
strikes.description: Deals 2d6 cold and 2 cold splash; on a hit the target takes a −5-foot status penalty to its Speeds until the end of its next turn.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Frost Vial (Greater)
variants.effect: 3d6 cold, 3 splash; −5 ft Speed on hit

strikes.name: Frost Vial
strikes.description: Deals 3d6 cold and 3 cold splash; on a hit the target takes a −5-foot status penalty to its Speeds until the end of its next turn.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Frost Vial (Major)
variants.effect: 4d6 cold, 4 splash; −5 ft Speed on hit

strikes.name: Frost Vial
strikes.description: Deals 4d6 cold and 4 cold splash; on a hit the target takes a −5-foot status penalty to its Speeds until the end of its next turn.

name: Full Plate
description: Plate mail consists of interlocking plates that encase nearly the entire body in a carapace of steel. It is costly and heavy, and the wearer often requires help to don it correctly, but it provides some of the best defense armor can supply. A suit of this armor comes with an undercoat of padded armor and a pair of gauntlets.

name: Gate Attenuator

variants.label: Standard
variants.effect: Grants a kineticist a +1 item bonus to impulse attack rolls. Activate (single action, envision) to cast a 1st-rank spell keyed to an element you channel (metal: thunderstrike) with a +7 spell attack modifier or DC 17.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Gate Attenuator (Greater)
variants.effect: The impulse attack bonus increases to +2. Activation casts a 5th-rank element spell (metal: impaling spike) at +18 / DC 28.

description: A crystalline focus worn against the sternum that stabilizes the elemental gate a kineticist channels, sharpening their impulses.

name: Gecko Potion
description: A gecko potion is a sticky, tawny brown liquid with flecks of sand suspended in it. For 5 minutes after drinking this potion, your fingertips sprout thousands of microscopic, bristled hairs that cling to objects, granting you a +1 item bonus to Climb and Palm an Object, and to your Reflex DC against Disarm attempts.

consumable.note: Climb/Palm (5 min)

name: Ghost Charge
description: A charge of positive energy especially harmful to the undead. Deals 1d8 vitality and 1 vitality splash; on a hit the target is enfeebled 1 until the start of your next turn.

strikes.name: Ghost Charge
strikes.description: Deals 1d8 vitality and 1 vitality splash; on a hit the target is enfeebled 1 until the start of your next turn.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Ghost Charge (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1d8 vitality, 1 splash; enfeebled 1 on hit

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Ghost Charge (Moderate)
variants.effect: 2d8 vitality, 2 splash; enfeebled 1 on hit

strikes.name: Ghost Charge
strikes.description: Deals 2d8 vitality and 2 vitality splash; on a hit the target is enfeebled 1 until the start of your next turn.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Ghost Charge (Greater)
variants.effect: 3d8 vitality, 3 splash; enfeebled 1 on hit

strikes.name: Ghost Charge
strikes.description: Deals 3d8 vitality and 3 vitality splash; on a hit the target is enfeebled 1 until the start of your next turn.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Ghost Charge (Major)
variants.effect: 4d8 vitality, 4 splash; enfeebled 1 on hit

strikes.name: Ghost Charge
strikes.description: Deals 4d8 vitality and 4 vitality splash; on a hit the target is enfeebled 1 until the start of your next turn.

name: Ghostcracker
description: A ghostcracker pops and smokes when consumed. When you throw the ghostcracker down in your space as part of casting an Illusory Creature spell, the appearance of the creature twists nightmarishly. When an enemy's attack or spell ends the illusory creature spell, the creature "dies" in a disturbing fashion, rendering the enemy Frightened 1. From this effect, the ghostcracker adds the emotion, fear, and mental traits to the spell.

catalyst.effect: When an enemy's attack or spell ends the spell, that enemy becomes Frightened 1 (emotion, fear, mental).

name: Glaive
description: A single-edged blade on a long pole. It deals 1d8 slashing damage and has the deadly d8, forceful, and reach traits.

actions.name: Recall Name
actions.description: Effect You stare at another creature and instantly remember their name if you've met and exchanged names. The glasses rely on your latent memories, so they don't penetrate a disguised identity.

name: Glasses of Sociability
description: These wire-rim glasses with large, circular lenses grant you a +1 item bonus to Diplomacy checks.

name: Gloom Blade
description: As black as coal, this blade grows more potent in darkness. While in bright light it functions as a +1 shortsword and doesn't radiate a magic aura to detect magic (unless the spell is 4th rank or higher). In dim light or darkness it becomes a +2 striking shortsword. Whenever you attack a creature you're undetected by, you deal 1d6 additional precision damage. Toggle the item's illumination mode to match the lighting.

name: Glow Rod

consumable.note: Bright light in a 20-ft radius (dim to 40 ft) for 6 hours.

description: When activated, this rod sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius (and dim light to the next 40 feet) for 6 hours. A glow rod can be loaded into a sun dazzler.

name: Glue Bomb
description: A bomb of fast-setting adhesive that mires its target. Deals no damage. On a hit the target takes a −10-foot status penalty to its Speed for 1 minute; on a critical hit it is immobilized for 1 round (Escape DC 17).

strikes.name: Glue Bomb
strikes.description: Deals no damage. On a hit the target takes a −10-foot status penalty to its Speed for 1 minute; on a critical hit it is immobilized for 1 round (Escape DC 17).

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Glue Bomb (Lesser)
variants.effect: No damage; −10 ft Speed; crit immobilized (Escape DC 17)

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Glue Bomb (Moderate)
variants.effect: No damage; −10 ft Speed; crit immobilized (Escape DC 17)

strikes.name: Glue Bomb
strikes.description: Deals no damage. On a hit the target takes a −10-foot status penalty to its Speed for 1 minute; on a critical hit it is immobilized for 1 round (Escape DC 17).

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Glue Bomb (Greater)
variants.effect: No damage; −15 ft Speed; crit immobilized (Escape DC 28)

strikes.name: Glue Bomb
strikes.description: Deals no damage. On a hit the target takes a −15-foot status penalty to its Speed for 1 minute; on a critical hit it is immobilized for 1 round (Escape DC 28).

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Glue Bomb (Major)
variants.effect: No damage; −20 ft Speed; crit immobilized (Escape DC 37)

strikes.name: Glue Bomb
strikes.description: Deals no damage. On a hit the target takes a −20-foot status penalty to its Speed for 1 minute; on a critical hit it is immobilized for 1 round (Escape DC 37).

name: Goo Grenade
description: A grenade of clinging alchemical goo. Deals 1d4 acid, 1 persistent acid, and 1 acid splash; the clinging goo imposes a −5 circumstance penalty to Speeds (Escape DC 15 or 3 Interact actions).

strikes.name: Goo Grenade
strikes.description: Deals 1d4 acid, 1 persistent acid, and 1 acid splash; the clinging goo imposes a −5 circumstance penalty to Speeds (Escape DC 15 or 3 Interact actions).

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Goo Grenade (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1d4 acid, 1 persistent acid, 1 splash; −5 Speed (Escape DC 15)

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Goo Grenade (Moderate)
variants.effect: 2d4 acid, 2 persistent acid, 2 splash; −5 Speed (Escape DC 15)

strikes.name: Goo Grenade
strikes.description: Deals 2d4 acid, 2 persistent acid, and 2 acid splash; the clinging goo imposes a −5 circumstance penalty to Speeds (Escape DC 15 or 3 Interact actions).

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Goo Grenade (Greater)
variants.effect: 3d4 acid, 3 persistent acid, 3 splash; −5 Speed (Escape DC 15)

strikes.name: Goo Grenade
strikes.description: Deals 3d4 acid, 3 persistent acid, and 3 acid splash; the clinging goo imposes a −5 circumstance penalty to Speeds (Escape DC 15 or 3 Interact actions).

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Goo Grenade (Major)
variants.effect: 4d4 acid, 4 persistent acid, 4 splash; −5 Speed (Escape DC 15)

strikes.name: Goo Grenade
strikes.description: Deals 4d4 acid, 4 persistent acid, and 4 acid splash; the clinging goo imposes a −5 circumstance penalty to Speeds (Escape DC 15 or 3 Interact actions).

name: Gourd Head
description: You have a large gourd for a skull, and since you don't have a physical brain, you can use the space inside your head. You can store a collection of up to 1 Bulk of objects within your head. The DC of checks to Steal objects from inside your head increases by 4. Additionally, if you store only one object within your head, you can draw it effortlessly into your hand as part of another action to use the object. Drawing the item grants this other action the manipulate trait.

name: Greataxe
description: This large battle axe is too heavy to wield with only one hand. Many greataxes incorporate two blades, and they are often "bearded," having a hook at the bottom to increase the strength of their chopping power.

name: Greatsword
description: This massive two-handed sword is nearly 5 feet in length. Its lower blade is often somewhat dulled to allow it to be gripped for extra leverage in close-quarter fights.

reactions.name: Drain Reversal
reactions.trigger: You gain the drained condition from an undead.
reactions.description: Frequency once per day; Effect Reduce the drained value by 1, and the triggering undead takes 2d6 vitality damage.

name: Grim Ring
description: While wearing this ring, you can detect the presence of undead during exploration (without pinpointing locations). The ring can't detect undead whose appearance is masked by an illusion spell of 2nd rank or higher. If an undead is hiding or disguised, the GM rolls a secret Perception check for you against the undead's Stealth or Deception DC, with a +2 item bonus.

name: Guisarme
description: A hooked blade on a long shaft. It deals 1d10 slashing damage and has the reach and trip traits.

name: Halberd
description: This polearm has a relatively short, 5-foot shaft. The business end is a long spike with an axe blade attached. Its reach lets you Strike enemies up to 10 feet away.

name: Half Plate
description: Half plate consists of most of the upper-body plates used in full plate, with lighter or sparser steel plate protection for the arms and legs. This provides some of the protection of full plate with greater flexibility and speed.

name: Hand of Mercy
description: Activate (one-action, manipulate). Shaped like an open-palmed hand, this small sculpture of smooth sandstone seems to blunt a weapon when applied rather than sharpen it. For 1 minute, a weapon to which a hand of mercy is applied gains the nonlethal trait and can't be used to make lethal attacks. Any persistent damage the weapon would deal is negated.

whetstone.reminder: Nonlethal; can't make lethal attacks; persistent damage the weapon would deal is negated.

name: Handwraps of Mighty Blows
description: These strips of cloth wrap around your hands and wrists. They can have fundamental and property runes etched onto them just like a weapon, and those runes apply to all of your unarmed attacks — your monk's unarmed Strikes included, not only the fist shown here. Upgrade, add, or transfer runes exactly as you would for a weapon; property runes apply only when appropriate to the unarmed attack you're using. Etched here at +1 striking; add a Shock rune later for the electricity theme.

name: Healer's Gel (Greater)
description: An astringent aroma from rare succulent plants wafts off these soothing cloth patches. Adding this material component to a Heal spell bestows an additional 15 temporary Hit Points to one target healed by the spell. These temporary Hit Points last for 1 minute.

catalyst.effect: One target healed by the spell also gains 15 temporary Hit Points for 1 minute.

name: Healer's Gel (Lesser)
description: An astringent aroma from rare succulent plants wafts off these soothing cloth patches. Adding this material component to a Heal spell bestows an additional 5 temporary Hit Points to one target healed by the spell. These temporary Hit Points last for 1 minute.

catalyst.effect: One target healed by the spell also gains 5 temporary Hit Points for 1 minute.

name: Healer's Gel (Moderate)
description: An astringent aroma from rare succulent plants wafts off these soothing cloth patches. Adding this material component to a Heal spell bestows an additional 10 temporary Hit Points to one target healed by the spell. These temporary Hit Points last for 1 minute.

catalyst.effect: One target healed by the spell also gains 10 temporary Hit Points for 1 minute.

actions.name: Healer's Touch
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Effect You soothe the wounds of a willing, living, adjacent creature, restoring 2d6+7 Hit Points to that creature. This is a healing vitality effect. You cannot harm undead with this healing.

name: Healer's Gloves
description: These clean, white gloves never show signs of blood, even after they're used to stitch up wounds or treat other ailments. They give you a +1 item bonus to Medicine checks.

name: Healer's Toolkit
description: This kit of bandages, herbs, and suturing tools is necessary for Medicine checks to Administer First Aid, Treat Disease, Treat Poison, or Treat Wounds. If you wear your healer's toolkit, you can draw and replace them as part of the action that uses them.

variants.label: Expanded
variants.name: Healer's Toolkit (Expanded)
variants.effect: Expanded healer's toolkits provide a +1 item bonus to Medicine checks to Administer First Aid, Treat Disease, Treat Poison, or Treat Wounds.

name: Healer's Toolkit (Expanded)
description: An expanded healer's toolkit contains higher-quality supplies. It grants a +1 item bonus to Medicine checks to Administer First Aid, Treat Disease, Treat Poison, or Treat Wounds.

name: Healing Potion

variants.label: Minor
variants.effect: The potion restores 1d8 Hit Points.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.effect: The potion restores 2d8+5 Hit Points.

variants.label: Moderate
variants.effect: The potion restores 3d8+10 Hit Points.

variants.label: Greater
variants.effect: The potion restores 6d8+20 Hit Points.

variants.label: Major
variants.effect: The potion restores 8d8+30 Hit Points.

description: A healing potion is a vial of a ruby-red liquid that imparts a tingling sensation as the drinker's wounds heal rapidly. When you drink a healing potion, you regain the listed number of Hit Points.

consumable.note: Restores HP — enter your rolled total

name: Heartstone
description: This small dense stone is wrapped in wire. When activated, it spreads across the face of your shield and up the arm that wields it, protecting it and its wearer for as long as the stone holds up against attack. Your shield gains 5d6 + 10 temporary Hit Points and you gain half that amount.

name: Heater Shield
description: This wooden or metal shield is crafted in an elongated heart shape that makes it one of the most versatile shields available. It is small enough to be used effectively from horseback, and often emblazoned with a coat of arms or heraldry on its face.

name: Hide Armor
description: A mix of thick furs and patches of stiff hide, hide armor provides protection due to its layers of leather, although its bulkiness slows the wearer down and decreases mobility.

name: Holy Water
description: This vial contains water blessed by a benevolent deity. You activate a vial of holy water by throwing it as a Strike. It's a simple thrown weapon with a range increment of 20 feet. Holy water deals 1d6 spirit damage and 1 spirit splash damage. Holy water can damage only creatures with the unholy trait.

name: Hoplon
description: This round metal shield affords great protection and durability. While it is the smallest of the heavy shields, its simple and durable construction makes it capable of withstanding many hits. Its smaller profile also makes it the most maneuverable of the heavy shields and unlike the other heavy shields, this prevents the use of the Take Cover action to gain an additional circumstance bonus to AC.

name: Horn of Blasting

actions.name: Sonic Blast
actions.description: Activate (one action, Interact); Frequency once per round; Effect One creature within 30 feet takes 3d6 sonic damage (basic Fortitude DC 28).

actions.name: Devastating Blast
actions.description: Activate (two actions, Interact); Frequency once per day; Effect A 30-foot cone deals 8d6 sonic damage (basic Fortitude DC 28). On a failure, a creature is also deafened for 2d6 rounds (apply the deafened condition by hand).

description: This ornate brass horn grants a +2 item bonus to Performance checks when played, and can loose devastating blasts of sound on command.

actions.name: Blow the Horn
actions.description: Once per hour, you can blow on the horn to unleash a swirling aura of jagged, rusty metal shards that orbit you in a 5-foot emanation for 1 minute. The shards grant you a +1 item bonus to AC. Creatures and objects that begin their turn in this area take 6d6 slashing damage from the shards (DC 19 basic Reflex save). A creature damaged by this aura is exposed to a poten form of Tetanus that is a Level 5 disease and has a DC 19 Fortitude Save. Worshippers of Xar-Azmak are immune to this effect.

actions.name: Consume Corruption
actions.description: Once per day, you can place the horn's bell against a diseased creature and then inhale into the mouthpiece rather than blow into it. The horn attempts to draw out the disease, casting Cleanse Affliction with a counteract modifier of +11. If the Horn of Rust succeeds at countering the disease, it gains a number of staff charges equal to half the disease's level rounded down. If it critically fails, not only is the disease not countered, but you are exposed to the disease and must attempt a saving throw against it to resist contracting the affliction yourself.

name: Horn of Rust
description: This rusted, coiled iron horn is sizable, appearing similar in shape to a ram's horn but measuring nearly 2 feet in diameter. Flakes of rust constantly fall from it, and the item leaves hands with a rusty red stain that lingers. While the Horn of Rust's power has diminished over the ages since Xar-Azmak's death, those who carry it are still subject to its palpable chaotic influences. If you are not sanctified Unholy, you are Enfeebled 1 while carrying or using the Horn of Rust; if you are sanctified Holy, you are instead Enfeebled 2 and Stupefied 1. It can be prepared as a staff, granting four spells.

name: Horrid Figurine

actions.name: Nauseating Visage
actions.description: Activate (two actions); Frequency twice per day; Effect Each creature in a 20-foot emanation attempts a DC 24 Fortitude save or is sickened 2 (you are immune). A third activation in a day dissolves the figurine into inert glue and leaves you sickened 3.

actions.name: Frightful Visage
actions.description: Activate (two actions); Frequency twice per day; Effect Each creature in a 20-foot emanation attempts a DC 24 Will save or is frightened 3 (you are immune). A third activation in a day dissolves the figurine into inert glue and leaves you sickened 3.

description: This grotesque statuette projects waves of nausea or terror. Each of its two visages can be invoked up to twice per day; a third invocation destroys it.

name: Howl of Winter
description: This spellgun somehow appears to be a naturally-formed pistol shaped icicle. You Activate the spellgun by aiming it at one creature and making your choice of a spell attack roll or a firearm attack roll against the target's AC. This spellgun has a range increment of 30 feet. The spellgun blasts bone-chilling arctic wind that deals cold damage based on the spellgun's type, then melts.

actions.name: Activate a Spellgun
actions.description:
Interact to level the spellgun at one creature, then Strike: make your choice of a spell attack roll or a firearm attack roll against the target's AC. Then the spellgun melts.

Critical Success The target takes double damage and takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speed.
Success As critical success, except the target takes full damage and takes a –5-foot status penalty.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Howl of Winter (Lesser)
variants.penalty: until the end of the target's next turn
variants.effect: 2d6 cold; the Speed penalty lasts until the end of the target's next turn

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Howl of Winter (Moderate)
variants.penalty: until the start of your next turn
variants.effect: 7d6 cold; the Speed penalty lasts until the start of your next turn

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Howl of Winter (Greater)
variants.effect: 12d6 cold; the Speed penalty lasts for 1 minute

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Howl of Winter (Major)
variants.effect: 16d6 cold; the Speed penalty lasts for 1 minute

name: Hunter's Bow
description: This +1 shortbow is favored by stealthy hunters and rogues. If you Strike a target that can't see you and score a critical hit, the target takes an extra 1d6 damage (in addition to any sneak attack damage).

riders.label: Hunter's Bow (vs unseen, crit)
riders.note: vs a target that can't see you

name: Ichthyosis Mutagen
description: Your skin thickens into overlapping scales that knit wounds closed and seal bleeding, but flake constantly and slow your reflexes — leaving an easy trail to follow.

consumable.note: Fast healing 2, auto-recover persistent bleed; −2 Reflex; trackable +4 for 24 hr (1 min)

actions.name: Cast Thoughtful Gift
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Range 120 feet; Targets 1 willing creature. You teleport one object of light or negligible Bulk held in your hand to the target. The object appears in the target's hand if they have a free hand, or at their feet if they don't. The spell fails if the target is unconscious, refuses your gift, or if the spell would teleport a creature.

name: Idolis Tattoo
description: This tattoo features traditional Varisian geometry and is part of an astrological set of symbol tattoos. Having one provides you with a +1 to Diplomacy checks when speaking to native Varisians, and allows you to cast Thoughtful Gift once per day. (Usage: tattooed on the body.)

name: Improved Mirror
description: A mirror that is custom-fitted for your shield. You gain access to the following reaction. If you use the Shield Block reaction, or gain the benefits of the Shield Block reaction with this shield in response to a critical hit from an attack, this adjustment is broken and you cannot benefit from it until it has been repaired as part of a successful Repair action on the shield.

actuated.name: Improved Mirror
actuated.description: Trigger You are targeted by an effect with the visual trait; Effect You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your AC or saving throw against the triggering effect. Regardless of whether you are affected by the triggering effect, you may attempt to redirect the effect at a target within 30 feet. If the effect requires a saving throw, the target must attempt the same saving throw. If it requires an attack roll, attempt a shield attack roll to determine whether it hits.

name: Injection Reservoir
usage: applied to a non-injection melee weapon that deals piercing damage
description: This reservoir and spring-loaded needle can be attached to a weapon to let it inject deadly poisons. Additionally, the reservoir can be filled with an injury poison. Immediately after a successful attack with the adjusted weapon, you can inject the target with the loaded poison by activating the reservoir with an Interact action. Refilling the reservoir with a new poison requires 3 Interact actions and uses both hands. Adding an injection reservoir to a weapon throws off its balance, causing the multiple attack penalty with the weapon to be one greater than usual (usually –6 on a second attack and –11 on a third; or –5 and –10 with an agile weapon).

name: Interior Polish
description: You have used special alchemical agents to polish the interior of your shield to a reflective shine, giving you a partial view of what is happening behind you. You gain access to the following action, which can only be used one time. After you have used the action you must spend 1 minute cleaning the interior of your shield to be able to use it again. If someone uses the Repair action on your shield they can clean the shield during the repair.

actuated.name: Interior Polish
actuated.description: Trigger An enemy attacks you and you have the flat-footed condition because they are flanking you or you are unaware of them. Effect You do not suffer the penalty of the flat-footed condition until the end of the current turn.

name: Inventor's Chair
description: A traveler's chair crafted by a gifted inventor who lost the use of their legs, it is a marvel of clockwork engineering that maneuvers about on its own, equipped with wheel blades and built-in sterling artisan's tools. As a relic it awakens further gifts: emotion surge and a Crafting muse (minor), emotion conduit and enthralling (major), and artistic perfection in Crafting (grand).

name: Jade Bauble

activation.trigger: You activate the bauble on the affixed weapon.

description: This carved jade charm is affixed to a melee weapon. When activated, until the start of your next turn all enemies within the reach of that weapon are off-guard.

name: Jade Cat
description: A talisman carved from jade in the shape of a cat, affixed to armor. Activate it (free action) when you fall or attempt an Acrobatics check to Balance (requires trained in Acrobatics). For 1 minute, you treat all falls as 20 feet shorter, you are not off-guard when you Balance, and narrow surfaces and uneven ground are not difficult terrain for you.

consumable.note: Falls 20 ft shorter; sure-footed Balance (1 min)

name: Juggernaut Mutagen
description: An alchemical mutagen that toughens the body, granting resilience and temporary vigor.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Juggernaut Mutagen (Lesser)
variants.effect: The bonus is +1 and you gain 5 temporary HP.

consumable.note: +1 Fort, 5 temp HP; −2 Will, Perception, initiative (1 min)

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Juggernaut Mutagen (Moderate)
variants.effect: The bonus is +2 and you gain 10 temporary HP.

consumable.note: +2 Fort, 10 temp HP; −2 Will, Perception, initiative (10 min)

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Juggernaut Mutagen (Greater)
variants.effect: The bonus is +3 and you gain 30 temporary HP.

consumable.note: +3 Fort, 30 temp HP; −2 Will, Perception, initiative (1 hr)

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Juggernaut Mutagen (Major)
variants.effect: The bonus is +4 and you gain 45 temporary HP.

consumable.note: +4 Fort, 45 temp HP; −2 Will, Perception, initiative (1 hr)

name: Katana
description: A curved, single-edged sword known for its wickedly sharp blade.

name: Key of Unwinding
description: A key of unwinding is a narrow brass clock hand, its pointed end fashioned into the shape of a key. When applied to a weapon, a key of unwinding forms a direct link to the Dimension of Time, allowing you to draw on a fragment of temporal power. For 1 minute, you gain the Rewrite Time reaction while wielding the attuned weapon. Rewrite Time (reaction) (magical, occult) Trigger You or an ally within your weapon's reach or range increment would be hit by a Strike or a spell attack roll Effect Attempt a Strike with the affected weapon against the triggering enemy. On a success, you deal no damage, but you negate the triggering attack or spell. Actions and spell slots used to attempt this attack are lost, and the triggering creature's multiple attack penalty increases as normal, as the results of the action are siphoned into the Dimension of Time. Any creature attempting to use Rewrite Time more than once per combat becomes Slowed 1 for 1 round after each subsequent use, as their own time begins to leech away.

whetstone.reminder: Grants the Rewrite Time reaction: when you or an ally in reach/range would be hit, Strike the attacker — on a success, negate the triggering attack or spell.

name: Kirin Echo Chime
description: The echo of kirin's song can be captured within this small glass chime, which shatters and calls up a meandering breeze of its own when rung. Adding this catalyst to a Gust of Wind spell causes any flying creatures that would be pushed by the spell's effects to be pushed 30 feet in a direction of your choice, rather than 30 feet in the direction of the spell.

catalyst.effect: Flying creatures pushed by the spell are pushed 30 feet in a direction of your choice.

name: Kite Shield
description: The kite shield is essentially a larger version of the heater shield, usually tapering more sharply at the bottom and being longer as well. It provides more full-body protection at the expense of maneuverability.

name: Kukri
description: The blade of this foot-long knife curves inward and lacks a cross guard at the hilt.

name: Lady's Knife
description: This +1 striking returning dagger has an elaborate, gemmed handle and can be strapped to the inside of a wrist or tucked within a bodice.

actions.name: Favor
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Effect You grant the blade to an ally, forming a link that lasts until your next daily preparations. While linked, you and your ally each gain the benefits of the status spell, using each other as the target.

actions.name: Recall
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Requirements You have an active Favor link; Effect The knife teleports instantly to your hand. This fails if the knife is more than 1 mile away. Your linked ally can also use this activation once per day to recall the knife.

name: Leather Armor
description: A mix of flexible and molded boiled leather, a suit of this type of armor provides some protection with maximum flexibility.

name: Leeching Fangs
description: Activate (one-action, manipulate). A set of leeching fangs is a metal sculpture in the form of a fanged maw, and when applied to a weapon, it causes the weapon or its ammunition to grow numerous mouths filled with serrated teeth. Leeching fangs establish a metaphysical link between the life force of the wielder of the weapon it's applied to and those it damages. Whenever you deal Hit Point damage to a living creature with a weapon under the effect of leeching fangs, you heal yourself half the amount of Hit Point damage dealt. This cannot heal you above your maximum number of Hit Points.

whetstone.reminder: Dealing HP damage to a living creature heals you half that amount (not above max HP).

name: Light Mace
description: A short flanged mace. It deals 1d4 bludgeoning damage and has the agile, finesse, and shove traits.

name: Limning Gem
description: Activate (one-action, manipulate). This luminescent white gem glows from within, and a weapon it's applied to gains its inner radiance, shedding dim light in a 10-foot radius. Successful Strikes with a weapon affected by a limning gem outline the target in a bright white light; if the target was Invisible, it becomes Concealed instead. This light lasts until the end of your next turn or for the remainder of the limning gem's duration on a critical hit.

onHit.note: Target outlined in bright white light until the end of your next turn (critical hit: for the gem’s remaining duration); an Invisible target becomes Concealed instead.

whetstone.reminder: Weapon sheds dim light (10 ft); hits outline the target in light until the end of your next turn.

name: Linguist's Dictionary

actuated.name: Absorb Language
actuated.description: Activate (free action, concentrate); Frequency once per day; Trigger Your last action cast a language-comprehension spell such as translate or truespeech; Effect The book absorbs one language, letting you communicate rudimentarily in it after the spell ends. It holds one language at a time.

description: This ever-updating polyglot grimoire drinks in languages. Once per day it can absorb one language from a comprehension spell you cast.

name: Longbow
description: This 5-foot-tall bow, usually made of a single piece of elm, hickory, or yew, has a powerful draw and is excellent at propelling arrows with great force and at an extreme distance. You must use two hands to fire a longbow, and it can't be used while mounted. Because it's made for long range, you take a -2 penalty to attacks on creatures within 30ft with it.

name: Longspear
description: This very long spear deals 1d8 piercing damage and has the reach trait.

name: Longsword
description: Longswords can be one-edged or two-edged swords. Their blades are heavy and they're between 3 and 4 feet in length.

name: Mace
description: With a stout haft and a heavy metal head, a mace is sturdy and reliable. Its weight and balance make it well suited to Shoving enemies off balance.

name: Machete
description: This broad, machete-style blade is a martial melee weapon in the sword group. It deals 1d6 slashing damage and has the deadly d8 and sweep traits.

name: Maestro's Instrument (Lesser)

actuated.name: Charming Performance
actuated.description: Activate (two actions, manipulate); Frequency once per day; Effect The instrument casts charm (DC 17).

description: This silver flute resonates with beguiling magic. You gain a +1 item bonus to Performance checks while playing music with it, and once per day its Charming Performance casts charm (DC 17).

name: Maestro's Instrument (Moderate)

actuated.name: Charming Performance
actuated.description: Activate (two actions, manipulate); Frequency once per day; Effect The instrument casts charm (4th rank, DC 27).

description: This masterwork instrument grants a +2 item bonus to Performance checks while you play it, and once per day its Charming Performance casts charm (4th rank, DC 27).

name: Magic Scroll
description: A scroll is a spell stored in written form. Casting the spell from a scroll requires Activating it (Cast a Spell), after which the scroll is consumed. A specific scroll is named for the spell it holds (Scroll of Heal, Scroll of Web), and derives its rank, item level, price, and Bulk from that spell. This base entry carries the shared scroll artwork that every such scroll displays.

name: Magic Wand
description: A magic wand holds a single spell, which you can cast once per day by Activating it (Cast a Spell), with the option to overcharge it for an additional casting at some risk. A specific wand is named for the spell it holds (Wand of Heal), and derives its rank, item level, price, and Bulk from that spell. This base entry carries the shared wand artwork that every such wand displays.

name: Magnetic Token

activation.trigger: You use Shield Block to reduce the damage from an attack made with a metal weapon.

description: This token seems to shift position around the shield periodically and unpredictably. When activated you cause your shield to become magnetic. You may immediately attempt to Disarm the blocked weapon and you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to this check.

name: Masquerade Scarf
description: This delicately embroidered scarf seems to match every outfit. Activate it (1 minute, manipulate; once per day) to arrange it over your lower face and cast a 1st-rank illusory disguise on yourself; the disguise ends immediately if the scarf is removed. The scarf's own appearance can be altered or made invisible, though it remains detectable by touch. (A greater version, item 7, activates in 2 actions any number of times per day and casts a 2nd-rank illusory disguise.)

name: Maul
description: Mauls are massive warhammers that must be swung with two hands.

name: Mesmerizing Opal

activation.trigger: You activate the opal and attempt a Deception check to Feint

effect.note: to Feint — a success becomes a critical success and a critical failure becomes a failure

description: This silver-bound opal pendant is afire with iridescence. When you activate it, attempt a Deception check to Feint. If the outcome is a success, you get a critical success instead; if it is a critical failure, you get a failure instead.

name: Midnight Milk
description: The strange, swirling liquid in this bottle is said to give those who injest it a feeling of drowsy euphoria as it lulls them to sleep. It is a new drug, making its way into surface markets via trade routes in the Darklands.

name: Midnight Milk (Experimental)
description: An unstable, experimental batch of midnight milk brewed by the Devil’s Disciples. Like ordinary midnight milk it lulls the drinker toward drowsy euphoria and sleep, but its afflictions are stronger and erratic — the GM adjudicates its drug and addiction effects.

name: Midnight Milk (Refined)
description: A refined, more potent batch of midnight milk brewed by the Devil’s Disciples. Saving Throw DC 29 Fortitude; Onset 1 minute; Maximum Duration 8 hours. Stage 1 +3 item bonus to Perception (1 hour); Stage 2 unconscious (1 hour); Stage 3 unconscious, plagued by nightmares (1 hour) and left fatigued on waking. The GM runs the drug and its addiction.

name: Mighty Counterweight
description: Activate (one-action, manipulate). This thick lead disk is etched with geometric patterns, and a weapon it's applied to grows unusually heavy. When you attack with a weapon under the effects of a mighty counterweight, you deal an amount of additional bludgeoning damage equal to the number of weapon damage dice.

whetstone.reminder: +1 bludgeoning damage per weapon damage die.

name: Mirror
description: A simple hand mirror that can be tied to a shield. You gain access to the following reaction, which causes the mirror to fall off of the shield when used. You may reattach the mirror to the shield as part of a Repair action, or by adding it to the shield again as normal. If you use the Shield Block reaction, or gain the benefits of the Shield Block reaction with this shield in response to a critical hit from an attack, this adjustment is destroyed and cannot be repaired.

actuated.name: Mirror
actuated.description: Trigger You would be required to make a saving throw against an effect with the visual trait, but have not rolled yet. Effect You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your saving throw against the triggering effect, and the mirror adjustment falls off of the shield.

name: Mirror Robe
description: Thousands of small, reflective, mirrored glass shards have been carefully stitched down this long silk duster.

actions.name: Mirrored Diversion
actions.description: Activate Interact; Requirements The mirror robe was last activated to draw attention toward you, or you haven't activated the mirror robe today; Effect The mirror robe Creates a Diversion for you, using a +9 modifier for the Deception check.

actions.name: Draw Attention
actions.description: Activate Interact; Requirements The mirror robe was last activated to divert attention away from you, and you are hidden from or undetected by at least one foe; Effect You choose one foe to whom you were hidden or undetected, reveal yourself to all creatures (becoming observed), and that foe becomes off-guard to your allies until the beginning of your next turn.

name: Mistform Elixir

variants.label: Lesser
variants.effect: A faint mist makes you concealed for 3 rounds.

variants.label: Moderate
variants.effect: A faint mist makes you concealed for 1 minute.

variants.label: Greater
variants.effect: A faint mist makes you concealed for 5 minutes.

description: Upon drinking this elixir, a faint mist emanates from your skin, making you concealed for the listed duration. Concealment does not let you Hide if your position is otherwise obvious.

consumable.note: Concealed (3 rounds / 1 min / 5 min)

name: Moderate Drakeheart Mutagen
description: Your skin grows scales like a drake, and your eyesight become sharp and your pupils slitted, but your mind and reflexes slow. You gain a +5 Item bonus to AC, a Dexterity cap of +2 (as usual, use your lowest Dexterity cap if you have more than one), and a +2 item bonus to Perception checks. If you're wearing armor, you still calculate your proficiency bonus to AC based on your proficiency in the armor you're wearing, even if the drakeheart mutagen has a higher item bonus. While under its effects, you take a –1 penalty to Will saves, Reflex saves, and all skill checks to Recall Knowledge. As a single action, you can burn through the remaining duration of the Mutagen to stride twice.

consumable.note: +5 item AC, +2 item Perception; Dex cap +2 & −1 Will/Reflex/RK penalties (see effect)

name: Monarch
description: Monarch functions as a +1 striking returning starknife that can't have additional property runes, as it's a specific item. Monarch deals an additional 1d6 untyped damage to creatures with the aberration trait — automatically added on a hit against an aberration. The rider does not apply while such a creature is disguised as another creature; untoggle it on the damage panel in that case. Monarch is a relic and has no market price.

actuated.name: Dream Message
actuated.description: Activate (10 minutes, envision, Interact); Frequency once per day; Requirements Monarch possesses a major gift; Effect Monarch casts dream message (heightened to 4th rank) to your specifications.

name: Moonlit Chain
description: This +1 silver chain shirt has a collar adorned with stitched images of the phases of the moon. You can see in moonlight as though you had low-light vision.

actions.name: Dim Sight
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Effect You touch the stitched image of the new moon on the armor's collar and suppress the dazzled condition for 1 minute.

name: Moonlit Spellgun
description: This spellgun emits a silvery ray of pure moonlight that deals fire damage depending on its type. Activate the spellgun by aiming it at one creature and making your choice of a spell attack roll or a firearm attack roll against the target's AC. This spellgun has a range increment of 30 feet. The spellgun deals more damage at night than at other times.

actions.name: Activate a Spellgun
actions.description:
Interact to level the spellgun at one creature, then Strike: make your choice of a spell attack roll or a firearm attack roll against the target's AC. It deals more damage at night than at other times. Then the spellgun fades.

Critical Success The target takes double damage and is dazzled until the start of your next turn. If it has a weakness to silver or a resistance that can be bypassed by silver, it's also enfeebled 1 for 1 minute.
Success The target takes full damage and is dazzled until the start of your next turn.

variants.label: Minor
variants.name: Moonlit Spellgun (Minor)
variants.effect: 1d8 fire at night, 1d6 at other times; dazzled until the start of your next turn

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Moonlit Spellgun (Lesser)
variants.effect: 3d8 fire at night, 3d6 at other times; dazzled until the start of your next turn

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Moonlit Spellgun (Moderate)
variants.effect: 7d8 fire at night, 7d6 at other times; dazzled until the start of your next turn

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Moonlit Spellgun (Greater)
variants.effect: 12d8 fire at night, 12d6 at other times; dazzled until the start of your next turn

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Moonlit Spellgun (Major)
variants.effect: 18d8 fire at night, 18d6 at other times; dazzled until the start of your next turn

name: Morph Jewel
description: Activate (one-action, manipulate). Constructed of an oddly flexible material, this golden jewel holds whatever shape it's twisted into. Choose bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing when you apply a morph jewel to a weapon. The weapon's damage becomes that type for 1 minute.

whetstone.reminder: The weapon's damage becomes the chosen type.

reactions.name: Amulet's Abeyance
reactions.trigger: The target of your Exploit Vulnerability would damage you or an ally within 15 feet of you and you are holding your implement.
reactions.description: You forcefully present your amulet to turn away harm. You or a target ally within 15 feet gain resistance to all damage against the triggering damage. The resistance is equal to 2 + your level.

name: Mother's Amulet
description: A blood-red ruby set in irridescent metal, the last gift your mother gave you.

name: Mud Bomb
description: A bomb of dense clinging mud. Deals 1d6 bludgeoning and 1 bludgeoning splash; on a critical hit the target is dazzled until the start of your next turn.

strikes.name: Mud Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 1d6 bludgeoning and 1 bludgeoning splash; on a critical hit the target is dazzled until the start of your next turn.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Mud Bomb (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1d6 bludgeoning, 1 splash; crit dazzled

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Mud Bomb (Moderate)
variants.effect: 2d6 bludgeoning, 2 splash; crit dazzled

strikes.name: Mud Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 2d6 bludgeoning and 2 bludgeoning splash; on a critical hit the target is dazzled until the start of your next turn.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Mud Bomb (Greater)
variants.effect: 3d6 bludgeoning, 3 splash; crit dazzled

strikes.name: Mud Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 3d6 bludgeoning and 3 bludgeoning splash; on a critical hit the target is dazzled until the start of your next turn.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Mud Bomb (Major)
variants.effect: 4d6 bludgeoning, 4 splash; crit dazzled

strikes.name: Mud Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 4d6 bludgeoning and 4 bludgeoning splash; on a critical hit the target is dazzled until the start of your next turn.

onCritConditions.note: until the start of your next turn

name: Mysterious Blue Orb
description: Streaks of blue shimmer within the smoky depths of this perfect crystalline sphere. Both the scintillating shade and emanations of an indefinable power suggest that something slumbers within. When all is quiet, Blu can feel it pulling at him.

name: Naginata
description: A curved single-edged blade mounted on a long shaft. It deals 1d8 slashing damage and has the deadly d8, reach, and versatile P traits.

name: +1 Dragon Bane Naginata
description: This +1 naginata is etched with a dragon bane rune. It deals an extra 1d6 damage to creatures with the dragon trait. (The rune also grants a +1 circumstance bonus to attack rolls against dragons — apply it by hand.)

name: Necrotic Bomb
description: A bomb of life-draining negative energy. Deals 1d6 void and 1 void splash; on a critical hit the target is sickened 1.

strikes.name: Necrotic Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 1d6 void and 1 void splash; on a critical hit the target is sickened 1.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Necrotic Bomb (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1d6 void, 1 splash; crit sickened 1

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Necrotic Bomb (Moderate)
variants.effect: 2d6 void, 2 splash; crit sickened 1

strikes.name: Necrotic Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 2d6 void and 2 void splash; on a critical hit the target is sickened 1.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Necrotic Bomb (Greater)
variants.effect: 3d6 void, 3 splash; crit sickened 1

strikes.name: Necrotic Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 3d6 void and 3 void splash; on a critical hit the target is sickened 1.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Necrotic Bomb (Major)
variants.effect: 4d6 void, 4 splash; crit sickened 1

strikes.name: Necrotic Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 4d6 void and 4 void splash; on a critical hit the target is sickened 1.

name: Nightpitch
description: A pellet of tar mixed with the fur of a nocturnal creature, such as a bat, nightpitch used to cast a Darkness spell makes the area of the darkness difficult terrain.

catalyst.effect: The darkness's area becomes difficult terrain.

name: Noxious Incense
description: Sold only in single sticks, this foul incense comes coated with a bevy of alchemical smoke that activates in the presence of intense heat, releasing its namesake pungent odor. Adding this catalyst to a Wall of Fire spell fills all squares adjacent to the wall with thick, foul smoke. Creatures in this area are concealed, and other creatures are concealed to creatures in the area. The smoke lasts for the duration of the spell. The incense can be used with a wall of fire of 6th level or below.

catalyst.effect: Fills squares adjacent to the wall with concealing smoke for the duration (wall of fire 6th level or below).

name: Noxious Incense (Greater)
description: Sold only in single sticks, this foul incense comes coated with a bevy of alchemical smoke that activates in the presence of intense heat, releasing its namesake pungent odor. Adding this catalyst to a Wall of Fire spell fills all squares adjacent to the wall with thick, foul smoke. Creatures in this area are concealed, and other creatures are concealed to creatures in the area. The smoke lasts for the duration of the spell. The incense can be used with a wall of fire of any level.

catalyst.effect: Fills squares adjacent to the wall with concealing smoke for the duration (wall of fire of any level).

name: Obsidian Goggles
description: These sleek goggles have black lenses that make everything seem more brightly lit, granting a +1 item bonus to Perception checks involving sight. Activate them (single action, manipulate; once per day) to gain darkvision for 1 hour. (Greater, item 11, gives a +2 bonus and unlimited use; major, item 18, gives +3 and greater darkvision.)

name: Octopus Bottle

save.note: A creature within reach 15 ft is repositioned unless it succeeds; in water the octopus then fills a 30-foot emanation with an ink cloud for 1 minute.

description: Uncork this bottle to loose a Huge octopus that grasps and repositions a creature within reach 15 feet unless it succeeds at a DC 24 Fortitude save. If it is in water, it then releases an ink cloud in a 30-foot emanation (creatures inside are concealed and can’t use scent) for 1 minute.

name: Oil of Weightlessness
description: You can spread this shimmering oil on an item of 1 Bulk or less to make it feel weightless. It has negligible Bulk for 1 hour.

consumable.label: Weightless
consumable.note: Negligible Bulk (1 hr)

name: Ommatophoric Mutagen
description: Upon activation your eyes bulge from their sockets and extend upward on long, prehensile stalks, greatly enhancing your eyesight and field of view but leaving you unable to close them.

consumable.note: +2 visual Perception, all-around vision (unflankable), low-light; −2 saves vs visual (1 min)

name: Clothing (Ordinary)
description: Ordinary, everyday clothing.

name: Padded Armor
description: This armor is simply a layer of heavy, quilted cloth, but it is sometimes used because it's so inexpensive. Padded armor is easier to damage and destroy than other types of armor. Heavy armor comes with a padded armor undercoat included in its Price, though it loses the comfort trait when worn under heavy armor. You can wear just that padded armor undercoat to sleep in, if your heavy armor is destroyed, or when otherwise not wearing the full heavy armor.

name: Panacea
description: When you drink this shimmering potion, it attempts to counteract all curses and diseases affecting you, as well as the blinded and deafened conditions from spells affecting you (counteract rank 7, +20 modifier).

name: Parade Armor
usage: affixed to medium or heavy armor
description: Parade armor is an armor adjustment that creates a set of pseudo-decorative armor to wear during noncombat functions. Any medium or heavy armor can be fashioned into parade armor, which features custom designs based on the wearer's affiliation, military branch, or rank. A particular faction typically has one uniform appearance for its set of parade armor. While wearing this armor, you gain a +1 item bonus to Diplomacy and Intimidation checks against creatures of the same affiliation as your parade armor, though the bonus to Intimidation doesn't apply when interacting with creatures of higher rank than you. The armor is slightly bulkier, increasing the Bulk by 1.

name: Pavise
description: This enormous rectangular metal shield can be used to protect the wielder's entire body, and is often used as a source of cover for allied archers, wielded both for the defense of the user and for nearby allies. It is incredibly heavy and unwieldy.

name: Pendant of the Occult
description: This amulet is hollow and shaped like an unblinking eye, its cavity typically holding a fragment of occult text. While wearing the pendant, you gain a +1 item bonus to Occultism checks and can cast guidance as an occult innate cantrip. (A greater version, item 9, grants a +2 bonus and a once-daily 4th-rank dream message.)

name: Penultimate Heartbeat
description: Activate (one-action, manipulate). This small hunk of slightly rusted iron gives off a faint reddish glow that occasionally pulses when touched. Tales of penultimate heartbeats say that the metal attracts a creature's spirit at the moment of death and channels it outward for a grisly display. Once applied to a weapon, a penultimate heartbeat's effects last for 1 hour. When you reduce a living creature to 0 Hit Points with a weapon under the effect of a penultimate heartbeat, it doesn't perish immediately (though is visibly on the brink of death). You can Reposition the creature up to 10 feet as a free action, after which it dies in a dramatic fashion appropriate to the injury that killed it. Foes in a 30-foot cone you direct originating from the creature's final position are sprayed with gore, taking 9d10 spirit damage (emotion fear mental) from the release of the creature's soul. An affected target attempts a Will save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. This effect has the emotion, fear, and mental traits. Critical Success The creature is unaffected. Success The creature takes half damage and is Frightened 1 from the shock. Failure The creature takes full damage and is Frightened 2. For the remainder of the penultimate heartbeat's duration, the creature can't reduce the value of the frightened condition below 1 if it ends its turn where it can see you. Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and is Frightened 3. For the remainder of the penultimate heartbeat's duration, the creature can't reduce the value of the frightened condition below 2 if it ends its turn where it can see you.

whetstone.reminder: Reducing a living creature to 0 HP: Reposition it up to 10 ft free, then it dies — 9d10 spirit damage (emotion, fear, mental) in a 30-ft cone you direct (basic Will vs the creature's level-based DC).

name: Pernicious Spore Bomb
description: A bomb that bursts into a cloud of barbed, toxic spores. Deals 1 poison on a direct hit, 1d4 persistent piercing, and 1 poison splash; one square of the target’s space becomes difficult terrain for 1 round.

strikes.name: Pernicious Spore Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 1 poison on a direct hit, 1d4 persistent piercing, and 1 poison splash; one square of the target’s space becomes difficult terrain for 1 round.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Pernicious Spore Bomb (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1 poison, 1d4 persistent piercing, 1 splash; difficult terrain

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Pernicious Spore Bomb (Moderate)
variants.effect: 2 poison, 2d4 persistent piercing, 2 splash; difficult terrain

strikes.name: Pernicious Spore Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 2 poison on a direct hit, 2d4 persistent piercing, and 2 poison splash; one square of the target’s space becomes difficult terrain for 1 round.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Pernicious Spore Bomb (Greater)
variants.effect: 3 poison, 3d4 persistent piercing, 3 splash; difficult terrain

strikes.name: Pernicious Spore Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 3 poison on a direct hit, 3d4 persistent piercing, and 3 poison splash; one square of the target’s space becomes difficult terrain for 1 round.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Pernicious Spore Bomb (Major)
variants.effect: 4 poison, 4d4 persistent piercing, 4 splash; difficult terrain

strikes.name: Pernicious Spore Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 4 poison on a direct hit, 4d4 persistent piercing, and 4 poison splash; one square of the target’s space becomes difficult terrain for 1 round.

name: Persona Mask

variants.label: Standard
variants.effect: +1 item bonus to Performance checks while acting, orating, performing comedy, or singing. Activate (single action, concentrate) — Assume Role: transform the mask into an artistic rendition of a dramatic character you select.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Persona Mask (Greater)
variants.effect: The bonus increases to +2, and you also gain Sacrifice Role (reaction, once per day): when you fail a Performance check benefiting from the mask, reroll and use the second result.

description: An expressive alabaster mask that covers the whole face without hindering vision or the other senses, favored by orators and stage performers.

name: Peshpine Grenade
description: A grenade packed with drug-laced pesh spines. Deals 1d6 piercing and 1 piercing splash; on a hit the target gains the stupefied condition until the end of its next turn.

strikes.name: Peshpine Grenade
strikes.description: Deals 1d6 piercing and 1 piercing splash; on a hit the target gains the stupefied condition until the end of its next turn.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Peshpine Grenade (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1d6 piercing, 1 splash; stupefied on hit

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Peshpine Grenade (Moderate)
variants.effect: 2d6 piercing, 2 splash; stupefied on hit

strikes.name: Peshpine Grenade
strikes.description: Deals 2d6 piercing and 2 piercing splash; on a hit the target gains the stupefied condition until the end of its next turn.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Peshpine Grenade (Greater)
variants.effect: 3d6 piercing, 3 splash; stupefied on hit

strikes.name: Peshpine Grenade
strikes.description: Deals 3d6 piercing and 3 piercing splash; on a hit the target gains the stupefied condition until the end of its next turn.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Peshpine Grenade (Major)
variants.effect: 4d6 piercing, 4 splash; stupefied on hit

strikes.name: Peshpine Grenade
strikes.description: Deals 4d6 piercing and 4 piercing splash; on a hit the target gains the stupefied condition until the end of its next turn.

name: Phasing Trine
description: Activate (one-action, manipulate). A phasing trine is a metallic triangle embedded with a clear crystal designed in the shape of a bow and nocked arrow. Strikes made with a ranged weapon under the effect of a phasing trine against a target who is observed by or Hidden to you (but not Undetected) pass through any non-magical barriers or walls in their way, though magical barriers stop the ammunition. These Strikes ignore all cover and circumstance bonuses to AC from shields. The Strike's damage can't be reduced with a Shield Block reaction using a non-magical shield.

whetstone.reminder: Ranged Strikes vs observed/hidden targets pass through non-magical barriers, ignore all cover and shield circumstance bonuses; non-magical Shield Blocks can't reduce the damage.

name: Philter of Empty Dreams

consumable.note: +1 item bonus to saves vs dream/nightmare effects; no dreams for 8 hours.

description: Drinking this cloudy philter grants you dreamless sleep for 8 hours and a +1 item bonus to saving throws against dream- or nightmare-themed effects (including midnight milk). If you are addicted to midnight milk, it attempts to counteract the addiction (counteract level 4, +15 modifier). An intellect devourer controlling a stolen body that drinks this must succeed at a DC 24 Fortitude save or be forced to Exit the Body.

name: Phoenix Tail Feather
description: This large feather shimmers between orange and blue and is noticeably warm to the touch. Adding this catalyst to a casting of blazing dive prevents allies from having to save against the damage caused by your landing, instead being granted 2 temporary Hit Points per rank that last until the end of your next turn.

catalyst.effect: Allies in the area don't save against your landing damage; each instead gains 2 temporary Hit Points per spell rank until the end of your next turn.

name: Pinpoint Arrowhead
description: Activate (one-action, manipulate). This metal arrowhead is sharp at the tip but never draws the blood of its user. When you attempt a ranged Strike with a weapon under the effect of a pinpoint arrowhead, you treat any cover the target has as one degree less (for example, treat standard cover as lesser cover, and lesser cover as no cover).

whetstone.reminder: Ranged Strikes treat the target's cover as one degree less (standard → lesser, lesser → none).

name: Pipes of Compulsion
description: The honeyed notes of these pipes coax and bend the will of those who listen. While playing the pipes, you gain a +1 item bonus to Diplomacy and Performance checks. You can Activate the pipes to Cast a Spell from its staff spell list.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Pipes of Compulsion (Greater)
variants.effect: Adds 2nd- and 3rd-rank spells to the staff.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Pipes of Compulsion (Major)
variants.effect: The item bonus while playing increases to +2. Adds 4th- and 5th-rank spells to the staff.

name: Pocketed Vest
description: A sturdy vest lined with pockets. Paired runes need one pocketed garment for each rune of the pair.

name: Poison Concentrator (Moderate)
description: Over a 10-minute activity, combine two doses of the same infused alchemical poison (level 9 or lower) into one concentrated dose that gains a +1 item bonus to its DC and increases in level by 1.

name: Potion of Flying (Standard)

consumable.note: Fly Speed 40 ft (1 min)

description: Upon drinking this effervescent concoction, you gain a fly Speed of 40 feet for 1 minute.

name: Potion of Waterbreathing
description: This filmy, gray potion reeks of ocean detritus and tastes even worse. After drinking this potion, you gain the ability to breathe underwater for 1 hour.

consumable.note: Breathe underwater (1 hr)

name: Power Ring
description: This seemingly simple metal band glows with mystic heat when magic travels through your fingers. You can add special ring runes to a power ring to further heighten your mastery over magic. When activated, it imbues your attack spells with increased accuracy. During your daily preparations, choose a single cantrip you can cast that would require a spell attack roll (more at higher grades). A chosen cantrip can ignore the frequency of the ring's activation, and using Veracious Spell with such a cantrip doesn't count toward its frequency for other spells. You cannot invest in more than one power ring. (Usage: worn; Bulk L.)

actions.name: Veracious Spell
actions.description: Frequency once per 10 minutes; Effect The ring's magic guides your attack. If the next action you use is to Cast a Spell that would require a spell attack roll, you gain the ring's item bonus to your spell attack roll. If the spell has multiple targets, the bonus applies to all of them. If a spell you modified with this effect could make additional spell attack rolls on a Sustain, the item bonus applies to these spell attack rolls as well.

variants.label: Iron
variants.name: Power Ring (Iron)
variants.description: The item bonus is +1. You can imbue one ring rune into the ring, and choose one cantrip during your daily preparations.

variants.label: Silver
variants.name: Power Ring (Silver)
variants.description: The item bonus increases to +2. You can choose up to 3 cantrips during your daily preparations. You can imbue two ring runes into the ring.

variants.label: Platinum
variants.name: Power Ring (Platinum)
variants.description: This power ring has the apex trait. When you invest it, you either increase your spellcasting attribute modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher score. The item bonus is +2. You can choose up to 5 cantrips during your daily preparations. You can imbue three ring runes into the ring.

name: Pressure Bomb
description: A bomb that detonates in a concussive blast of force. Deals 1d4 bludgeoning and 1 bludgeoning splash; on a critical hit the target is knocked prone, and a Tiny or smaller target is pushed 5 feet.

strikes.name: Pressure Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 1d4 bludgeoning and 1 bludgeoning splash; on a critical hit the target is knocked prone, and a Tiny or smaller target is pushed 5 feet.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Pressure Bomb (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1d4 bludgeoning, 1 splash; crit prone

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Pressure Bomb (Moderate)
variants.effect: 2d4 bludgeoning, 2 splash; crit prone

strikes.name: Pressure Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 2d4 bludgeoning and 2 bludgeoning splash; on a critical hit the target is knocked prone, and a Tiny or smaller target is pushed 5 feet.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Pressure Bomb (Greater)
variants.effect: 3d4 bludgeoning, 3 splash; crit prone

strikes.name: Pressure Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 3d4 bludgeoning and 3 bludgeoning splash; on a critical hit the target is knocked prone, and a Tiny or smaller target is pushed 5 feet.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Pressure Bomb (Major)
variants.effect: 4d4 bludgeoning, 4 splash; crit prone

strikes.name: Pressure Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 4d4 bludgeoning and 4 bludgeoning splash; on a critical hit the target is knocked prone, and a Tiny or smaller target is pushed 5 feet.

onCritConditions.note: a Tiny or smaller target is also pushed 5 feet

name: Primeval Mistletoe
description:
This sprig of berry-festooned holly and mistletoe doesn't wilt or rot. It can be used as a primal locus, and it grants a +1 item bonus to Nature checks while you wear it.

Activate—Anoint (two actions, manipulate; once per 10 minutes): Smear berry juice onto a wooden weapon to cast runic weapon on it, or onto a creature to cast runic body on it.

Activate—Bind (two actions, manipulate; once per day): Touch the sprig, then a tree, to cast one with plants upon yourself, turning into a vine on the touched tree.

name: Prismatic Crystal

activation.trigger: You are targeted by, or in the area of an effect that deals acid, cold, electricity, fire, poison, or sonic damage.

description: A small crystal that refracts light into its constituent colors, wrapped in a leather cord that can be tied to your shield. When activated, for 1 minute your shield transforms into an elemental version of the triggering damage type. During this effect, you may use your Shield Block reaction when you would take damage from the triggering damage type in addition to physical damage, and your shield is immune to the triggering damage type.

name: Psychopomp Mask
description: These minor magic items are fashioned as masks depicting your likeness or some metaphorical depiction thereof, to encourage the ushers of death to take the mask in your place.

freeActions.name: Cheat Death
freeActions.trigger: You begin your turn with a dying value of 3 or greater
freeActions.description: You forgo your recovery check. Instead, you lose the dying condition but remain unconscious at 0 Hit Points. The mask then cracks in half and is destroyed. The psychopomps won't be so easily fooled again — you become temporarily immune to the effects of any psychopomp mask for 1 year.

name: Quenching
description: This rune counters burning and corrosive agents. Armor with this rune reduces the DC of the flat check to end persistent acid or fire damage affecting you from 15 to 12 (7 with particularly effective assistance).

name: Quicksilver Mutagen
description: An alchemical mutagen that makes you swifter and nimbler, but more fragile.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Quicksilver Mutagen (Lesser)
variants.effect: The item bonus is +1 and the Speed bonus is +5 feet.

consumable.note: +1 Acro/Stealth/Thievery/Reflex/Dex-attacks, +5 ft Speed; −2 Fort (1 min)

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Quicksilver Mutagen (Moderate)
variants.effect: The item bonus is +2 and the Speed bonus is +10 feet.

consumable.note: +2 Acro/Stealth/Thievery/Reflex/Dex-attacks, +10 ft Speed; −2 Fort (10 min)

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Quicksilver Mutagen (Greater)
variants.effect: The item bonus is +3 and the Speed bonus is +15 feet.

consumable.note: +3 Acro/Stealth/Thievery/Reflex/Dex-attacks, +15 ft Speed; −2 Fort (1 hr)

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Quicksilver Mutagen (Major)
variants.effect: The item bonus is +4 and the Speed bonus is +20 feet.

consumable.note: +4 Acro/Stealth/Thievery/Reflex/Dex-attacks, +20 ft Speed; −2 Fort (1 hr)

actions.name: Cast Concordant Choir
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Range 30 feet; Saving Throw basic Fortitude. You unleash a dangerous consonance of reverberating sound. 1 action: 1d4 sonic damage to a single enemy. 2 actions (manipulate): 2d4 sonic damage to all creatures in a 10-foot burst. 3 actions (manipulate): 2d4 sonic damage to all creatures in a 30-foot emanation.

name: Ragario Tattoo
description: This tattoo features traditional Varisian geometry and is part of an astrological set of symbol tattoos. Having one provides you with a +1 to Diplomacy checks when speaking to native Varisians, and allows you to cast Concordant Choir once per day. (Usage: tattooed on the body.)

name: Ranseur
description: This polearm is a long trident with a central prong that is longer than the other two.

name: Rapier
description: The rapier is a long and thin piercing blade with a basket hilt. It is prized among many as a dueling weapon.

name: Rations
description: While they don't taste amazing, rations offer enough nutrition to keep you from starving. Each pack can feed a single person for a day.

name: Reactive Flash
description: Activate (one-action, manipulate). These whetstones are miniature stone replicas of weapons, such as a sword or an axe, though their function is the same regardless of their shape. When you attempt a Strike as a reaction (such as with a Readied action or the Reactive Strike reaction) using a weapon under the effect of a reactive flash, the target must first succeed at a DC 19 Reflex save or be Off-Guard against the attack.

whetstone.reminder: Strikes made as a reaction: target saves Reflex DC 19 first or is Off-Guard against the attack.

name: Ready
description: A ready rune lets you don or remove the etched armor in only a few seconds rather than minutes.

actions.name: Don or Remove Armor
actions.description: Effect You don or remove the armor.

name: Redpitch Bomb
description: A bomb of sticky, slow-burning red pitch. Deals 1 fire on a direct hit, 1d4 persistent fire, and 1 fire splash; on a critical hit the target is clumsy 1 until the start of your next turn.

strikes.name: Redpitch Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 1 fire on a direct hit, 1d4 persistent fire, and 1 fire splash; on a critical hit the target is clumsy 1 until the start of your next turn.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Redpitch Bomb (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1 fire, 1d4 persistent fire, 1 splash; crit clumsy 1

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Redpitch Bomb (Moderate)
variants.effect: 2 fire, 2d4 persistent fire, 2 splash; crit clumsy 1

strikes.name: Redpitch Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 2 fire on a direct hit, 2d4 persistent fire, and 2 fire splash; on a critical hit the target is clumsy 1 until the start of your next turn.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Redpitch Bomb (Greater)
variants.effect: 3 fire, 3d4 persistent fire, 3 splash; crit clumsy 1

strikes.name: Redpitch Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 3 fire on a direct hit, 3d4 persistent fire, and 3 fire splash; on a critical hit the target is clumsy 1 until the start of your next turn.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Redpitch Bomb (Major)
variants.effect: 4 fire, 4d4 persistent fire, 4 splash; crit clumsy 1

strikes.name: Redpitch Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 4 fire on a direct hit, 4d4 persistent fire, and 4 fire splash; on a critical hit the target is clumsy 1 until the start of your next turn.

onCritConditions.note: until the start of your next turn

name: Reinforced Surcoat
usage: applied to medium or heavy armor
description: This surcoat is made of thick fabric and light chain, designed to protect vital areas. When you are critically hit by an attack, you gain physical resistance equal to 2 + the value of the armor's potency rune against the attack's damage. If the armor is in the chain armor group and you have its armor specialization effect, you instead increase the physical resistance from the chain armor specialization by 2. This can't reduce the damage to less than the damage rolled for the hit before doubling for a critical hit. However, the reinforced surcoat increases the Speed penalty of your armor by 5 feet.

name: Reinforced Tower Shield
description: These massive shields can be used to provide cover to nearly the entire body. Due to their size, they are typically made of wood reinforced with metal. You provide cover for allies behind you when you raise a tower shield, and can increase the bonus to AC from +2 to +4 by Taking Cover after Raising the shield.

name: Reinforcing
description: A reinforcing rune bolsters a shield's structure, raising its Hardness, Hit Points, and Break Threshold up to a maximum for each grade.
