A shield's reinforcing grade also sets how many property runes it can hold: minor or lesser 1, moderate or greater 2, major or supreme 3. Shields don't take potency runes.

variants.label: Minor
variants.name: Minor Reinforcing
variants.description: Hardness +3 (max 8), HP +44 (max 64), BT +22 (max 32).

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Lesser Reinforcing
variants.description: Hardness +3 (max 10), HP +52 (max 80), BT +26 (max 40).

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Moderate Reinforcing
variants.description: Hardness +3 (max 13), HP +64 (max 104), BT +32 (max 52).

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Greater Reinforcing
variants.description: Hardness +5 (max 15), HP +80 (max 120), BT +40 (max 60).

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Major Reinforcing
variants.description: Hardness +5 (max 17), HP +84 (max 136), BT +42 (max 68).

variants.label: Supreme
variants.name: Supreme Reinforcing
variants.description: Hardness +7 (max 20), HP +108 (max 160), BT +54 (max 80).

name: Wooden Religious Symbol
description: A carved wooden symbol of a deity, used as a divine focus for casting divine spells.

name: Resilient
description: Resilient runes imbue armor with additional protective magic, granting the wearer an item bonus to saving throws.

variants.label: Resilient
variants.name: Resilient
variants.description: This grants the wearer a +1 item bonus to saving throws.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Resilient (Greater)
variants.description: The armor grants a +2 item bonus to saving throws.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Resilient (Major)
variants.description: The armor grants a +3 item bonus to saving throws.

name: Ring of Counterspells

reactions.name: Counterspell
reactions.trigger: You are targeted by or within the area of the spell stored in the ring.
reactions.description: You attempt to counteract the triggering spell, using the stored spell’s rank and a +19 counteract modifier. The ring must have a spell stored in it (a spellcaster can spend an action to store one spell of their choice; only one at a time).

description: A spellcaster can store a single spell in this ring. When you are targeted by or caught in the area of that same spell, the ring can attempt to counteract it (rank as stored, +19 counteract modifier).

name: Ring of Observation
description: This gold ring is engraved with eyes and inlaid with a single amber-colored cat-eye gemstone. While the gemstone itself isn't magical, it gives off the eerie impression that it's watching you. A Firebrand uses this ring to determine whether they've drawn the attention of locals and guards without giving away that they know they're being watched.

actions.name: Watchful Eye
actions.description: Activate envision; Frequency once per day; Effect The watchful eye of the cat-eye gemstone surveys your surroundings for 1 minute. During this time, you can use a single action with the concentrate trait to focus on the ring, and it grows warm to the touch if you're being intentionally observed by creatures within 30 feet. The ring can only notice creatures that aren't hidden from you and that are intentionally watching you.

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Ring of Observation (Moderate)
variants.effect: The survey lasts 10 minutes and detects observers within 60 feet. You can also cast invisibility on yourself once per day.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Ring of Observation (Greater)
variants.effect: The survey lasts 1 hour and detects observers within 120 feet. You can cast invisibility or 4th-rank invisibility on yourself once per day.

actions.name: Track Sigil
actions.description: Frequency once per 10 minutes; Effect You detect the general direction toward the most recent sigil you created using the ring. This fails if the sigil is more than 5 miles away or if there's lead or running water between you and the sigil.

actions.name: Cast Sigil
actions.description: You cast sigil as an arcane innate cantrip, leaving your personal mark.

name: Ring of Sigils
description: This silver band is carved with the personal sigils of different individuals, adding one to represent you when you invest it. It lets you cast sigil as an arcane innate cantrip.

variants.label: Standard
variants.name: Ring of Sigils
variants.effect: Track Sigil has a maximum range of 5 miles.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Ring of Sigils (Greater)
variants.effect: Track Sigil's maximum range is 100 miles. If the sigil was placed on a living creature, you also determine whether the creature is alive, its distance from you, and any conditions affecting it.

actions.name: Ram's Blast
actions.description: Frequency once per minute; Effect A ram-shaped blast of force slams into one foe you can see within 60 feet. The number of actions spent (1 to 3) determines the intensity: the blow deals 2d6 force damage per action spent and pushes the target 5 feet per action spent, with a DC 22 Fortitude save. Critical Success unaffected; Success half damage and pushed half the distance; Failure full damage and pushed the full distance; Critical Failure double damage and pushed twice the distance.

name: Ring of the Ram
description: This heavy iron ring is shaped to look like the head of a ram, with curling horns.

variants.label: Standard
variants.name: Ring of the Ram
variants.effect: 2d6 force damage per action spent; DC 22 Fortitude save.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Ring of the Ram (Greater)
variants.effect: The ring deals 3d6 force damage per action spent and the save DC is 32. When activated with 3 actions, you can disperse the force into multiple rams, targeting all creatures in a 30-foot cone instead of one target within 60 feet.

name: Ring of Wizardry (Type I)
description: This ring is made from the purest platinum and is covered in esoteric arcane symbols. It does nothing unless you have a spellcasting class feature with the arcane tradition. While wearing the ring, you gain a +1 item bonus to Arcana checks and have two additional 1st-rank arcane spell slots each day, which you can prepare or cast spontaneously as you normally cast your spells. If you take off the ring, you lose the additional spell slots. You can't gain spell slots from more than one ring of wizardry per day.

name: Rod of Wonder

actions.name: Activate Rod of Wonder
actions.description: Choose a creature within 60 feet and roll on the rod’s wonder table to determine a random effect (spell DC 27, spell attack +17). The rod can’t be activated again for 1d4 hours. Roll the table at the table.

description: This whimsical rod produces a random magical effect each time it’s activated. Its unpredictable results are rolled on the wonder table when used.

name: Rope (50ft)
description: Sturdy rope for tying something (or someone) down.

name: Runestone
description: This flat piece of hard stone is specially prepared for etching a magical fundamental rune or property rune. You can etch only one rune upon a stone. When a rune is transferred from the runestone to another object, the runestone cracks and is destroyed. The Price listed is for an empty stone; a stone holding a rune adds the Price of the rune. This base entry carries the shared runestone artwork that every runestone displays.

name: Rust Scrub
description: This salt scrub is infused with citron juice and alchemical ingredients that allow it to fight rust on metallic equipment. One application can be used to restore 2d4 Hit Points to an item that has been damaged by rust. The GM determines what portion of damage to an item was caused by rust if that value isn't known.

consumable.note: Restore 2d4 HP to rust damage (GM adjudicates)

name: Rusty, Broken Full Plate Armor
description: Large red rubies you pried from a statue of Xar-Azmak.

name: Salve of Antiparalysis

variants.label: Salve of Antiparalysis
variants.effect: The salve attempts to counteract the paralysis (counteract rank 3rd, counteract modifier +22).

variants.label: Greater
variants.effect: If the creature is petrified, it returns to normal, and for removing paralysis the salve's counteract modifier is +31 and its counteract rank is 6th.

description: Applying this filmy salve to a creature helps it overcome magical paralysis. Activate (one-action, manipulate); held in 2 hands. The salve attempts to counteract the paralysis (counteract rank 3rd, counteract modifier +22).

name: Sanguine Mutagen
description: An alchemical mutagen that boosts blood production, filtering contagions and bolstering endurance.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Sanguine Mutagen (Lesser)
variants.effect: The bonus is +1 (+2 vs disease/poison/fatigue).

consumable.note: +1 Fort & Reflex (+2 vs disease/poison/fatigue); 1d6 bleed when cut (1 min)

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Sanguine Mutagen (Moderate)
variants.effect: The bonus is +2 (+3 vs disease/poison/fatigue).

consumable.note: +2 Fort & Reflex (+3 vs disease/poison/fatigue); 1d6 bleed when cut (10 min)

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Sanguine Mutagen (Greater)
variants.effect: The bonus is +3 (+4 vs disease/poison/fatigue).

consumable.note: +3 Fort/Reflex (+4 vs disease/poison/fatigue); 1d6 bleed when pierced or slashed (1 hr)

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Sanguine Mutagen (Major)
variants.effect: The bonus is +4.

consumable.note: +4 Fort/Reflex; success→crit success vs disease/poison/fatigue; 1d6 bleed when pierced or slashed (1 hr)

activation.trigger: You attempt a save vs the initial exposure to poison or disease

name: Sanitizing Pin
description: This pin includes a piece of gauze wrapped around a dried herbal poultice. When you activate the sanitizing pin, the poultice dissolves and bolsters your body's defense against poison or disease. You gain a +2 status bonus on your Fortitude save against the initial exposure to the affliction. If you roll a critical failure on the triggering attempt, you get a failure instead.

name: Scale Mail
description: Scale mail consists of many metal scales sewn onto a reinforced leather backing, often in overlapping rows. The individual pieces are approximately the size of fish scales.

name: Scarf
description: A long woven scarf.

name: Scepter of Energy Ablation
description: Scepters of this type are often made of heavy iron and have large hand guards similar to some swords.

actions.name: Energy Ablation
actions.description: If your next action is to Cast a Spell that deals at least one type of energy damage (acid, cold, electricity, fire, force, sonic, vitality, or void), you gain resistance to one energy type the spell could deal equal to the spell's rank until the end of your next turn.

chain.modifier: If the spell deals energy damage, gain resistance to one energy type it could deal equal to the spell's rank until the end of your next turn.

actuated.name: Energy Abjection
actuated.description: You grant resistance to one energy damage type of your choosing to all allies within 30 feet, equal to double the rank of the spell you expended. This lasts for a number of rounds equal to the rank of the spell you expended.

name: Scepter of Greater Distances
description: Sometimes mistaken for small staves, these scepters are made of bamboo or other fast-growing plants.

actions.name: Reach Spell
actions.description: If the next action you use is to Cast a Spell that has a range, increase that spell's range by 30 feet. As is standard for increasing spell ranges, if the spell normally has a range of touch, you extend its range to 30 feet. If you have the Reach Spell feat, you increase the range of the spell by 45 feet instead of 30.

actuated.name: Mobilize the Distance
actuated.description: You grant all allies within 30 feet a +10-foot status bonus to Speed and a +1 status bonus to DCs and checks against effects that would impart immobilized, grabbed, or restrained for a number of rounds equal to the rank of the spell you expended.

name: Scepter of Heightening
description: Often made of polished burled wood, this scepter allows the user to reach new heights of power, using the energy from one spell to temporarily make many of their spells more powerful.

actions.name: Heighten
actions.description: Frequency once per hour; Effect If the next action you use is to Cast a Spell of at least 3 ranks below the highest-rank spell slot you have, you treat the rank of the spell as 2 higher for the purposes of numerical effects based on its heightened rank, such as damage, healing, increased area, bonuses, or penalties. You do not gain any other effects associated with the heightened rank, such as being able to target more creatures or treating the spell as a higher rank for the purposes of counteracting or the incapacitation trait.

chain.modifier: Numeric effects (damage, healing, area, bonuses) treat the spell as 2 ranks higher — not for counteract, incapacitation, or targeting. Once per hour; the spell must be at least 3 ranks below your highest slot.

actuated.name: Height of Your Power
actuated.description: For the duration, you can use the Heighten spellshape from this scepter as if it had a frequency of once per round instead of once per hour. This effect lasts for a number of rounds equal to the rank of the spell you expended.

name: Scepter of Purifying
description: You can tell this scepter by its effervescent-smelling handle.

actions.name: Purifying Spell
actions.description: Frequency once per hour; Effect If the next action you use is to Cast a Spell that would restore Hit Points to a single creature, you can attempt to counteract the target's sickened condition in addition to the normal effects, using the rank of the spell you cast for your counteract modifier.

actuated.name: Purifying Wave
actuated.description: You restore 1d8 Hit Points per rank of the spell to all creatures within a 30-foot cone. You can attempt to counteract the sickened condition of all creatures in the area, or a disease or poison affecting a single target in the area, using the rank of the spell you expended as the spell rank for your counteract modifier.

name: Scepter of Quickening
description: Scepters of this type are almost always made from stabilized quicksilver, polished so that it glistens in the faintest light. Despite this, they always appear slightly out of focus, as if they are moving too fast to be seen in one glance.

actions.name: Quickened Casting
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Effect If the next action you use is to Cast a cantrip or a spell that is at least 2 ranks lower than the highest-rank spell slot you have, reduce the number of actions to Cast it by 1 (minimum 1 action).

chain.modifier: Reduce the actions to Cast the spell by 1 (minimum 1). Once per day; the spell must be a cantrip or at least 2 ranks below your highest slot.

actuated.name: Hasten to Fight
actuated.description: You and all allies within 30 feet are quickened for 1 minute and can use the extra action each round only to Strike and Stride.

name: Scepter of Second Chances
description: The birch these scepters are made from sheds its paper-like bark as if it were still a living tree. The bark disappears quickly, leading some wielders to write secret desires on it and watch them vanish, in hopes that they will come back in reality.

actions.name: Dangerous Odds
actions.description: Frequency once per hour; Effect If the next action you use is to Cast a Spell that would target a single creature and requires a Will save, you can use your reaction if the target rolls a success on their save to force the creature to reroll their save and take the new result. This is a misfortune effect.

actuated.name: Manifest Desires
actuated.description: You and allies within 30 feet can manifest a better reality for yourselves for a number of rounds equal to the rank of the spell you expended. For the duration, affected creatures can choose to reroll a single attack roll, saving throw, or skill check. They must take the new result even if it is worse. This is a fortune effect.

name: Scepter of Sickening
description: These scepters are often green and brown, have an unpleasant, greasy feeling when touched, or carry a faint yet repulsive odor. Using the scepter to enhance a spell adds a bit of disgusting magic to it, leaving targets of the magic feeling sick.

actions.name: Sicken Spell
actions.description: Frequency once per hour; Effect If your next action is to Cast a Spell with a basic Fortitude save, creatures that fail their save are also sickened 1 (sickened 2 on a critical failure). The creature is affected whether or not the spell deals damage.

chain.modifier: Creatures that fail the spell's basic Fortitude save are also sickened 1 (sickened 2 on a critical failure), whether or not the spell deals damage.

actuated.name: Rotting Aura
actuated.description: You exude a putrid aura in a 20-foot emanation. The aura lasts a number of rounds equal to the rank of the expended spell. Creatures that end their turn within the area become sickened 1 or increase the value of their sickened condition by 1, to a maximum of sickened 4. Creatures in the area cannot reduce their sickened condition below 1.

name: Scimitar
description: This curved single-handed sword deals 1d6 slashing damage and has the forceful and sweep traits.

name: Scorpion Whip
description: A whip with a series of razor-sharp blades set along its tip. Unlike a normal whip it lacks the nonlethal trait, making it deadlier but less suited to taking foes alive.

name: Scutum
description: An ancient style of shield, but still effective, the scutum is large and rectangular, with a convex shape to help deflect incoming blows rather than absorb them into the shield. It is primarily made of wood and typically reinforced with metal bands. Famously used in shield formations, this shield is no less effective when used alone.

name: Scythe
description: A long farming blade repurposed for war. It deals 1d10 slashing damage and has the deadly d10 and trip traits.

name: Sea Touch Elixir (Moderate)

consumable.note: Swim Speed 20 ft and breathe underwater for 1 hour.

description: Your fingers and toes become webbed, granting you a swim Speed of 20 feet and the ability to breathe underwater for 1 hour.

name: Sense-Dulling Hood (Lesser)

consumable.note: +2 item to saves vs auditory/olfactory/visual effects; −1 Perception. 10 minutes.

description: Pulling on this muffling hood grants a +2 item bonus to saving throws against auditory, olfactory, and visual effects, but imposes a −1 penalty to Perception, for 10 minutes.

name: Serene Mutagen
description: An alchemical mutagen that calms the mind, sharpening senses and will at the cost of aggression.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Serene Mutagen (Lesser)
variants.effect: The item bonus is +1 (+2 to Will vs mental).

consumable.note: +1 Will/Perception/Med/Nat/Rel/Survival; −1 attacks & offensive spell DCs & damage/die (1 min)

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Serene Mutagen (Moderate)
variants.effect: The item bonus is +2 (+3 to Will vs mental).

consumable.note: +2 Will/Perception/Med/Nat/Rel/Survival; −1 attacks & offensive spell DCs & damage/die (10 min)

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Serene Mutagen (Greater)
variants.effect: The item bonus is +3.

consumable.note: +3 Will/Perception/Medicine/Nature/Religion/Survival; −1 attacks & spell DC, −1/die damage (1 hr)

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Serene Mutagen (Major)
variants.effect: The item bonus is +4.

consumable.note: +4 Will/Perception/Medicine/Nature/Religion/Survival; −1 attacks & spell DC, −1/die damage (1 hr)

name: Serpent Dagger

actuated.name: Drip Poison
actuated.description: Activate (free action, manipulate); Frequency once per day; Trigger You damage a creature with the serpent dagger; Effect You coat the blade in dagger venom. Dagger Venom (poison): Saving Throw DC 21 Fortitude; Maximum Duration 4 rounds; Stage 1 1d8 poison damage and enfeebled 1 (1 round).

description: This serpentine +1 striking dagger drips venom on command. On a critical hit, the target becomes sickened 1 unless it succeeds at a DC 19 Fortitude save (the GM rolls the save). Once per day, Drip Poison coats the blade with dagger venom when you damage a creature.

name: Seventh Prism (Pentagonal)
description: Beloved by the church of the Seventh Veil, a seventh prism is a crystal that disperses its internal light, casting an aurora of color. If you use a seventh prism to cast Dizzying Colors, targets are dazzled for twice as long as their saving throw indicates. On a critical failure, the target is dazzled for 1 minute after its blinded condition ends. Motes of shifting rainbow hues cloud the eyes, making it difficult to see details. The area increases to a 20-foot cone, and the DC for the flat check against the target being concealed increases to DC 7 flat check.

catalyst.effect: As the triangular prism, and the area becomes a 20-foot cone with the concealment flat check raised to DC 7.

name: Seventh Prism (Triangular)
description: Beloved by the church of the Seventh Veil, a seventh prism is a crystal that disperses its internal light, casting an aurora of color. If you use a seventh prism to cast Dizzying Colors, targets are dazzled for twice as long as their saving throw indicates. On a critical failure, the target is dazzled for 1 minute after its blinded condition ends. Motes of shifting rainbow hues cloud the eyes, making it difficult to see details.

catalyst.effect: Targets are dazzled for twice as long; on a critical failure, dazzled 1 minute after the blinded condition ends.

name: Shadow
description: Shadow runes make armor lighter and softer. While wearing the etched armor, you gain a +1 item bonus to Stealth checks.

name: Shadow Essence
description: Injury poison. Saving Throw DC 29 Fortitude; Maximum Duration 6 rounds. Stage 1 3d6 void and 2d6 poison (1 round); Stage 2 as Stage 1 plus enfeebled 1; Stage 3 as Stage 1 plus enfeebled 2. Enfeebled from this poison lasts 24 hours. The GM runs the affliction on a hit.

activation.trigger: You attempt to Escape — gain a +1 item bonus to the Acrobatics check; a success becomes a critical success

name: Shark Tooth Charm
description: This dried-seaweed bracelet is lined with charms shaped like small shark teeth. When you activate the bracelet, attempt to Escape using Acrobatics with a +1 item bonus to the check. If you roll a success, you get a critical success instead (if you roll a critical failure, you get a failure instead). If you fail the Acrobatics check against a grabbing creature, the creature must either release you as a free action or take 2d8 piercing damage as shark's teeth momentarily emerge from your skin.

name: Shield Augmentation
description: There are numerous methods to modify shields-snarling rods to catch weapons, bladed edges, padding for nonlethal strikes, and so on-but all share basic functionality. A shield augmentation can be etched with weapon runes, much like a shield boss or shield spikes, but doesn't otherwise alter your shield's statistics. A shield bearing an augmentation can't be combined with an attached weapon, like shield spikes. A shield augmentation grants your shield one or more weapon traits, chosen when the augmentation is created. You can either choose to add the backswing or forceful trait, or you can choose two of the following weapon traits: disarm, nonlethal, shove, thrown 10 feet, trip, or versatile S.

name: Shield Boss
description: A thick metal knob at the center of a shield, increasing the bludgeoning damage of a shield bash. Attach it to a shield (a 10-minute activity); its Strike is available while that shield is held, and it can be etched with its own weapon runes.

name: Shield Gauntlet
description: This gauntlet is finely crafted from thin steel plates that uniquely cover both the inside and outside of the hand. This careful construction allows for relatively fine control with the gauntleted hand, but also grants full protection for grabbing an enemy weapon by the dangerous end. You can wield weapons and carry objects in the hand that is wearing the shield gauntlet, but you aren't considered to be wielding it while you do so, nor can you Raise a Shield or use any Shield Reactions until the hand is once again free.

name: Shield Harness
usage: applied to a medium or heavy shield
description: Your shield has been modified so that you can harness it firmly to your back when you are not actively wielding it. When you are wearing your shield instead of wielding it, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC against attacks made against you if you are considered flat-footed against the attack because you are flanked by another enemy. If you are hit by such an attack, you take the damage as normal. The shield also takes the damage but reduces it by its Hardness. If the shield is broken or destroyed, you no longer receive this benefit.

name: Shield Sheath
description: You have added a bracket or sheath to your shield that allows you to stow your weapon. A weapon sheath can contain a single one-handed weapon whose bulk cannot exceed the bulk of the shield. When you draw your shield, if you have a free hand and there is a weapon in the shield's weapon sheath, you may draw that weapon with the same Interact action you use to draw the shield. Stowing a weapon in the sheath requires a single Interact action. This adjustment cannot be applied to a shield with the accessible trait.

name: Shield Spikes
description: Metal spikes placed on the striking face of a shield, dealing piercing damage with a shield bash. Attach it to a shield (a 10-minute activity); its Strike is available while that shield is held, and it can be etched with its own weapon runes.

name: Shield Strap
usage: applied to a light or medium shield
description: You have attached a leather or chain strap to your shield that you can loop over your forearm or a shoulder. When you Release your shield it doesn't fall to the ground and is considered to be worn. You may draw it as normal after Releasing it. The strap has a quick release, allowing you to still use actions and reactions that require throwing your shield, such as Interrupting Shield, but reattaching the strap is a three-action activity with the manipulate trait.

name: Shortbow
description: This smaller bow is made of a single piece of wood and favored by skirmishers and cavalry.

name: Shortsword
description: These blades come in a variety of shapes and styles, but they are typically 2 feet long.

name: Shuriken
description: This “throwing star” is a small piece of flat metal with sharp edges, designed to be flung with a flick of the wrist.

name: Sickle
description: A curved farming blade. It deals 1d4 slashing damage and has the agile, finesse, and trip traits.

name: Silver Orb
description: An orb of liquid silver that bursts into a clinging, glittering cloud. Deals 1 slashing on a direct hit, then bursts into a 5-foot silver cloud for 1 minute: creatures inside are concealed and take 1d6 persistent slashing each round (Fortitude DC 19 basic), and weapons and unarmed attacks made within it count as silver until cleaned.

strikes.name: Silver Orb
strikes.description: Deals 1 slashing on a direct hit, then bursts into a 5-foot silver cloud for 1 minute: creatures inside are concealed and take 1d6 persistent slashing each round (Fortitude DC 19 basic), and weapons and unarmed attacks made within it count as silver until cleaned.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Silver Orb (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1 slashing; cloud 1d6 slashing (Fort DC 19 basic), concealed

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Silver Orb (Greater)
variants.effect: 1 slashing; cloud 5d6 slashing (Fort DC 39 basic), concealed

strikes.name: Silver Orb
strikes.description: Deals 1 slashing on a direct hit, then bursts into a 5-foot silver cloud for 1 minute: creatures inside are concealed and take 5d6 slashing each round (Fortitude DC 39 basic), and weapons and unarmed attacks made within it count as silver until cleaned.

name: Silvertongue Mutagen
description: An alchemical mutagen that hones charm and guile while dulling scholarly recall.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Silvertongue Mutagen (Lesser)
variants.effect: The item bonus is +1.

consumable.note: +1 Deception/Diplomacy/Intimidation/Performance; −2 knowledge skills, lose a trained skill (1 min)

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Silvertongue Mutagen (Moderate)
variants.effect: The item bonus is +2.

consumable.note: +2 Deception/Diplomacy/Intimidation/Performance; −2 knowledge skills, lose a trained skill (10 min)

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Silvertongue Mutagen (Greater)
variants.effect: The item bonus is +3.

consumable.note: +3 Deception/Diplomacy/Intimidation/Performance; −2 knowledge skills, lose a trained skill (1 hr)

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Silvertongue Mutagen (Major)
variants.effect: The item bonus is +4.

consumable.note: +4 Deception/Diplomacy/Intimidation/Performance; −2 knowledge skills, lose a trained skill (1 hr)

freeActions.name: Loosen Lock
freeActions.description: Once per day, the key casts Knock on a Lock you're trying to pick, granting a +4 status bonus to attempts to pick it.

secret.name: Shrieking Key
secret.description: A skull-topped metal shrieking key appears to be a skeleton key, useful in place of thieves' tools when attempting to Pick a Lock. A shrieking key has no activation, however. When you use it to Pick a Lock, the key emits a loud shriek audible for 500 feet despite ambient noise. Physical barriers still block or muffle the shriek as normal. You also take a –2 circumstance penalty to the Thievery check rather than enjoying a bonus. After you attempt such a check with the key the first time, it magically attaches to you, returning to your possession if discarded. To use another device to Pick a Lock, you must first succeed at a DC 20 Will save.

name: Skeleton Key
description: A grinning skull tops the bow of this macabre key. This key can be used in place of a thieves' toolkit when attempting to Pick a Lock, and it grants a +1 item bonus to the Thievery check. If the skeleton key becomes broken due to a critical failure on the check, it works as a normal thieves' toolkit and loses its benefits until repaired.

actions.name: Administer Stitch
actions.description: You Administer First Aid without requiring healer's tools. You can either gain a +2 item bonus to the Medicine check or use the salve's Medicine modifier of +13 instead of your own.

freeActions.name: Stitch Wounds
freeActions.trigger: You Treat Wounds or use Battle Medicine.
freeActions.description: You gain a +2 item bonus to the triggering Medicine check. If you roll a success on the Medicine check, you get a critical success instead.

name: Skinstitch Salve
description: This sticky salve stubbornly holds wounds closed and encourages swift natural healing.

name: Slayer's Stone
description: Activate (one-action, manipulate). This dull red stone is shot through with glittering veins of black and silver. When you apply a slayer's stone to your weapon, choose aberration, animal, beast, celestial, construct, dragon, elemental, fey, fiend, giant, monitor, ooze, or both fungus and plant. A weapon under the effect of a slayer's stone deals an additional 1d6 precision damage to the creatures with the chosen trait or traits for 1 minute. It's up to the GM's discretion whether this benefit applies against a creature disguised as a creature with the chosen trait or traits.

whetstone.reminder: +1d6 precision damage vs creatures with the chosen trait.

name: Sleep Arrow
description:
Sleep arrows often have shafts of deep blue or black, and their fletching is exceptionally soft and downy. An activated sleep arrow deals no damage, but a living creature hit by it is subject to the effects of a sleep spell (DC 17).

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.
Success The creature takes a –1 status penalty to Perception checks for 1 round.
Failure The creature falls unconscious. If it's still unconscious after 1 minute, it wakes up automatically.
Critical Failure The creature falls unconscious. If it's still unconscious after 1 hour, it wakes up automatically.

failure.note: doesn't fall prone or release what it's holding; wakes automatically if still unconscious after 1 minute, or earlier on a successful Perception check

criticalFailure.note: doesn't fall prone or release what it's holding; wakes automatically if still unconscious after 1 hour, or earlier on a successful Perception check

ammunition.note: Sleep spell rider — on a Success the target takes a –1 status penalty to Perception checks for 1 round.

name: Sleeves of Storage
description:
This loose robe has wide, voluminous sleeves that each contain an extradimensional space. These spaces each function as a spacious pouch that can hold up to 5 Bulk of items (for a total of 10 Bulk), though no individual item can be of more than 1 Bulk; the sleeves grow slightly heavy as you reach maximum capacity. You can add or remove an item from a sleeve with a single hand free as an Interact action.

If a sleeve is completely empty, you can place your own familiar into that extradimensional space. It can survive comfortably in your sleeve for up to 1 hour, after which it begins to suffocate. While in your sleeve, it can't be affected or targeted by any effects, but you don't benefit from any master abilities. A familiar can exit the sleeve of its own volition with a single action that has the manipulate and move traits. You can't place any other creature into your sleeves, nor can you place your familiar in a sleeve if it's larger than Tiny. If your familiar is in your sleeve, you can't place any items in the sleeve.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Sleeves of Storage (Greater)
variants.effect: Each sleeve can hold up to 20 Bulk of items (40 Bulk total), and a familiar can survive within a sleeve for up to 4 hours.

name: Slick
description: The defenses provided by this rune make it harder for foes to grab you. While wearing the etched armor, you gain a +1 item bonus to Acrobatics checks to Escape and to Squeeze.

name: Sling
description: Little more than a leather cup attached to a pair of straps, a sling can be used to fling smooth stones or sling bullets at a range. Propulsive: add half your Strength modifier (if positive) to damage. Reloading takes 1 Interact action.

name: Sneaky Key

activation.trigger: You turn the key to activate it

effect.note: to Pick a Lock for 1 minute; your first success also grants an additional success toward opening a complex lock

description:
This small silver skeleton key can be pinned to armor or a sleeve. When you turn the key to activate it, you gain a +1 status bonus to Thievery checks to Pick a Lock for 1 minute. The first time you get a success or critical success to Pick a Lock, you achieve one additional success toward opening a complex lock.

Requirements You are trained in Thievery.

name: Soap
description: Scrub a dub dub

name: Soothing Tonic
description: This pleasantly savory concoction speeds your natural healing, so your wounds recover faster over time. You gain fast healing for 1 minute in an amount depending on the tonic's type. Activate (1 action, manipulate).

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Soothing Tonic (Lesser)
variants.effect: You gain fast healing 1.

consumable.note: Fast Healing 1 (1 min)

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Soothing Tonic (Moderate)
variants.effect: You gain fast healing 3.

consumable.note: Fast Healing 3 (1 min)

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Soothing Tonic (Greater)
variants.effect: You gain fast healing 5.

consumable.note: Fast Healing 5 (1 min)

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Soothing Tonic (Major)
variants.effect: You gain fast healing 10.

consumable.note: Fast Healing 10 (1 min)

name: Soulspeaker
description: This grotesque, amulet-like shrunken head is said to hold a shard of its owner's psyche. The head is often that of a humanoid, but any creature capable of speaking in life can provide the grisly component needed for this item's creation. While a soulspeaker carries a message, the eyes of the shrunken head open, and they close as soon as its message is delivered.

actions.name: Store Message
actions.description: Activate command; Effect You cast message, but instead of your message being transferred directly to a target's ears, it is stored in the soulspeaker and the head's eyes open. The next time the soulspeaker is activated, it repeats the stored message in a rasping whisper before its eyes close again and the message is expended.

name: Spacious Pouch
description: Though it appears to be a cloth bag decorated with panels of richly colored silk or stylish embroidery, a spacious pouch opens into a magical space larger than its outside dimensions. The Bulk held inside the bag doesn't change the Bulk of the spacious pouch itself. The amount of Bulk the bag's extradimensional space can hold depends on its type. You can Interact with the spacious pouch to stow items in it or remove them just like a mundane sack. Though the bag can hold a great amount of material, an object still needs to be able to fit through the opening of the sack to be stored inside. If the bag is overloaded or broken, it ruptures and is ruined, causing the items inside to be lost forever. If it's turned inside out, the items inside spill out unharmed, but the bag must be put right before it can be used again. A living creature placed inside the bag has enough air for 10 minutes before it begins to suffocate, and it can attempt to Escape against a DC of 13. An item inside the bag provides no benefits unless it's retrieved first. An item in the bag can't be detected by magic that detects only things on the same plane.

name: Spacious Pouch (Type II)
description: Though it appears to be a cloth bag decorated with panels of richly colored silk, a spacious pouch opens into a magical space larger than its outside dimensions. The Bulk held inside the bag does not change the Bulk of the pouch itself. You can Interact with the pouch to stow items in it or remove them just like a mundane sack, though an object still needs to fit through the opening. If the bag is overloaded or broken, it ruptures and is ruined, causing the items inside to be lost forever. A living creature placed inside has enough air for 10 minutes before it begins to suffocate, and it can attempt to Escape against a DC of 13.

name: Sparkblade
description: This +1 shortsword is forged of cold iron and etched with lightning. Cold iron weapons deal additional damage to creatures with weakness to cold iron, such as demons and fey.

actions.name: Lightning Arc
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Effect You point the sparkblade at a foe within 30 feet and shoot an arc of lightning that can leap to a second creature within 30 feet of the first. Each target takes 2d4+4 electricity damage (DC 19 basic Reflex save).

name: Sparking Spellgun
description: This spellgun shoots a searing bolt of flame that clings and burns. Activate the spellgun by aiming it at one creature and making your choice of a spell attack roll or a firearm attack roll against the target's AC. This spellgun has a range increment of 30 feet. The spellgun deals fire damage based on the spellgun's type plus persistent fire damage, then crumbles to ash.

actions.name: Activate a Spellgun
actions.description:
Interact to level the spellgun at one creature, then Strike: make your choice of a spell attack roll or a firearm attack roll against the target's AC. Then the spellgun crumbles to ash.

Critical Success The target takes double damage, takes double persistent damage, is blinded for 1 round, and is dazzled while the persistent damage lasts.
Success The target takes full damage, full persistent damage, and is dazzled while the persistent damage lasts.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Sparking Spellgun (Lesser)
variants.effect: 4d6 fire, 1d4 persistent fire, dazzled while the persistent damage lasts

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Sparking Spellgun (Moderate)
variants.effect: 7d6 fire, 3d4 persistent fire, dazzled while the persistent damage lasts

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Sparking Spellgun (Greater)
variants.effect: 10d6 fire, 5d4 persistent fire, dazzled while the persistent damage lasts

name: Spear
description: A long metal shaft ending with a metal spike, a spear can be used one-handed as a melee weapon and can be thrown.

actions.name: Comprehend Writing
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Effect Unfolding the spectacles onto the bridge of your nose, you gain the effects of 2nd-rank comprehend language for 1 hour, except that it applies to all languages you see rather than a single language, and it does not apply to language you hear.

name: Spectacles of Understanding
description: While wearing the spectacles, you gain the listed item bonus to checks to Decipher Writing.

variants.label: Standard
variants.name: Spectacles of Understanding
variants.effect: +1 item bonus to Decipher Writing; the comprehend language effect lasts 1 hour.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Spectacles of Understanding (Greater)
variants.effect: +2 item bonus to Decipher Writing; the comprehension lasts until you fold up the spectacles or the item is no longer invested by you, rather than 1 hour.

name: Spellslasher
description: Activate (one-action, manipulate). A spellslasher is made of faceted crystal, its edges sharp and oddly misaligned. When it's applied to a weapon, it attunes the weapon to magical energies, allowing it to slice through spells as though they were physical threads. While your weapon is under the effect of a spellslasher, you gain the Spellslash reaction; you can use this reaction only with the affected weapon, and the spellslasher's effects end as soon as you use Spellslash. Spellslash (reaction) (magical, manipulate) Trigger You are the only target of a spell Effect You swing or shoot your weapon to intercept the magic, attempting to counteract the spell. Your counteract rank is equal to half your level (rounded up), and your counteract check modifier is equal to your attack modifier with the affected weapon.

whetstone.reminder: Grants the Spellslash reaction: when you're the sole target of a spell, counteract it with a weapon swing (counteract modifier = your attack modifier); ends the whetstone.

name: Spiked Chain
description: A 4-foot length of chain covered with barbs and spikes, sometimes fitted with metal hoops as handgrips.

name: Spiked Steel Shield
description: Like wooden shields, steel shields come in a variety of shapes and sizes. Though more expensive than wooden shields, they are much more durable.

name: Spined Shield

actions.name: Loose Spine
actions.description: Activate (one action, Interact); Effect You shoot one of the shield’s spines at a target as a martial ranged weapon with a 120-foot range increment, using the spikes’ statistics. Each spine can only be fired once until the shield regenerates them the next day.

description: A steel shield (Hardness 6, HP 24, BT 12) bristling with five jagged +1 striking spikes you can Strike with. When the shield would take damage after its Hardness, one spine snaps off per 6 damage, reducing the damage taken by 6; with no spines left it takes damage normally and can’t Strike until the spines regenerate the next day.

name: Splint Mail
description: This type of armor is chainmail augmented with pauldrons to protect the shoulders and faulds around the waist. The pauldrons are made up of overlapping steel plates or splints of metal similar to scale mail.

name: Spoiling Buckler
description:
A wooden buckler made of magically preserved rotting wood. It smells foul, but is a token of friendship from the fey of Tickwood.

Spoiling: Any food within 5 feet of it spoils at double the usual rate.

Bullet Guard: You gain resistance 5 against firearms and slings.

actions.name: Tumbling Tumbleweed
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Effect the buckler spawns a spherical wicker cage that tumbles at one creature within 30 feet. The creature must succeed at a DC 19 Reflex save or be carried 15 feet in a direction of your choice.

name: Staff
description: This long piece of wood can aid in walking and deliver a mighty blow. When gripped in two hands (Two-Hand d8), its Strikes deal 1d8 damage.

name: Staff of Phantasms
description: An ornately designed metal staff inlaid with gold; when you Cast a Spell from it, the illusory image of something you desire flashes across its surface. While wielding the staff, you gain a +2 status bonus to checks to disbelieve an illusion.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Staff of Phantasms (Greater)
variants.effect: Adds 3rd- and 4th-rank castings of the staff's illusions (e.g., illusory creature at 4th rank).

name: Staff of the Unblinking Eye
description: The stone head of this staff is carved to look like a lidless eye. While wielding it, you gain a +1 status bonus to Perception checks for initiative, and you can Activate it to Cast a Spell from its staff spell list (detect magic; sure strike; darkvision, see the unseen, translate). Greater (item 10) and major (item 14) versions add higher-rank divination spells.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Staff of the Unblinking Eye (Greater)
variants.effect: Adds 3rd- and 4th-rank divination spells to the staff.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Staff of the Unblinking Eye (Major)
variants.effect: Adds 5th- and 6th-rank divination spells to the staff.

name: Stalwart's Ring
description: This fairly simple ring has no gemstone inlaid, but the band is cast to resemble a fanged wolf's head. Once per day, during your daily preparations, you can meditate on this ring to grant yourself 5 temporary Hit Points. These last until your next daily preparation, as long as you are wearing the ring.

name: Star Grenade
description: A grenade that detonates in a cross-shaped burst of fire. Deals 1d8 fire and 1 fire splash, bursting in a cross of two perpendicular 25-foot lines centered on the target.

strikes.name: Star Grenade
strikes.description: Deals 1d8 fire and 1 fire splash, bursting in a cross of two perpendicular 25-foot lines centered on the target.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Star Grenade (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1d8 fire, 1 splash; cross-shaped burst

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Star Grenade (Moderate)
variants.effect: 2d8 fire, 2 splash; cross-shaped burst

strikes.name: Star Grenade
strikes.description: Deals 2d8 fire and 2 fire splash, bursting in a cross of two perpendicular 25-foot lines centered on the target.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Star Grenade (Greater)
variants.effect: 3d8 fire, 3 splash; cross-shaped burst

strikes.name: Star Grenade
strikes.description: Deals 3d8 fire and 3 fire splash, bursting in a cross of two perpendicular 25-foot lines centered on the target.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Star Grenade (Major)
variants.effect: 4d8 fire, 4 splash; cross-shaped burst

strikes.name: Star Grenade
strikes.description: Deals 4d8 fire and 4 fire splash, bursting in a cross of two perpendicular 25-foot lines centered on the target.

name: Steel Shield
description: Like wooden shields, steel shields come in a variety of shapes and sizes. Though more expensive than wooden shields, they are much more durable.

name: Sterling Artisan's Tools (Talismans)
description: An incredibly high-quality set of artisan tools, perfectly suited to creating Talismans. If you use them when you Craft a Talisman, you get a +1 item bonus to the check.

name: Sticky Algae Bomb
description: A bomb of bioluminescent algae that tags and trails its target. Deals 1d8 poison and 1 poison splash; the target is tagged and leaves a highly visible trail for 1 hour (DC 19 to Track, but the trail shows only in water).

strikes.name: Sticky Algae Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 1d8 poison and 1 poison splash; the target is tagged and leaves a highly visible trail for 1 hour (DC 19 to Track, but the trail shows only in water).

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Sticky Algae Bomb (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1d8 poison, 1 splash; leaves a glowing trail (track DC 19, in water)

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Sticky Algae Bomb (Moderate)
variants.effect: 2d8 poison, 2 splash; leaves a glowing trail (track DC 19, in water)

strikes.name: Sticky Algae Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 2d8 poison and 2 poison splash; the target is tagged and leaves a highly visible trail for 1 hour (DC 19 to Track, but the trail shows only in water).

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Sticky Algae Bomb (Greater)
variants.effect: 3d8 poison, 3 splash; leaves a glowing trail (track DC 19, in water)

strikes.name: Sticky Algae Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 3d8 poison and 3 poison splash; the target is tagged and leaves a highly visible trail for 1 hour (DC 19 to Track, but the trail shows only in water).

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Sticky Algae Bomb (Major)
variants.effect: 4d8 poison, 4 splash; leaves a glowing trail (track DC 19, in water)

strikes.name: Sticky Algae Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 4d8 poison and 4 poison splash; the target is tagged and leaves a highly visible trail for 1 hour (DC 19 to Track, but the trail shows only in water).

name: Stone and Mortar
description:
A small piece of a brick with surrounding mortar that is wrapped in gold wire. When activated, you create a brick wall 3 feet tall, 1 foot thick, and 15 feet wide. The wall must be adjacent to you and centered on your square.

The wall is difficult terrain for creatures moving through it, and it provides standard cover to anyone in its area or adjacent to it. It can potentially provide cover for creatures further away at the GM's discretion.

name: Stone Body Mutagen
description: This mutagen calcifies your body, making it as hard as stone — turning aside blades and points at the cost of speed and agility.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Stone Body Mutagen (Lesser)
variants.effect: Resistance 5 to physical (except bludgeoning); −10 ft Speed; −2 Reflex.

consumable.note: Resistance 5 to physical (except bludgeoning); −10 ft Speed; −2 Reflex (10 min)

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Stone Body Mutagen (Moderate)
variants.effect: Resistance 5 to physical (except bludgeoning); −10 ft Speed; −2 Reflex.

consumable.note: Resistance 5 to physical (except bludgeoning); −10 ft Speed; −2 Reflex (1 hr)

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Stone Body Mutagen (Greater)
variants.effect: Resistance 10 to physical (except bludgeoning); −10 ft Speed; −2 Reflex.

consumable.note: Resistance 10 to physical (except bludgeoning); −10 ft Speed; −2 Reflex (1 hr)

name: Stone Body Mutagen (Lesser)
description: This mutagen calcifies your body, making it as hard as stone — turning aside blades and points at the cost of speed and agility.

consumable.note: Resistance 5 to physical (except bludgeoning); −10 ft Speed; −2 Reflex (10 min)

name: Storage
usage: applied to armor or unarmored defense clothing
description: The storage adjustment fits the armor or clothing with belts, buckles, pouches, and loops for holding and storing tools. Countless fasteners make the armor as jangly as chain mail. While wearing armor with this adjustment, you can wear up to 3 Bulk of toolkits instead of the usual 2. However, the armor acquires the noisy trait. If it already has the noisy trait, increase its penalty to Stealth checks by 1.

name: Storm Arrow
description:
The head of this arrow is made from gleaming copper. When an activated storm arrow hits a target, it's buffeted by raging winds and struck by a bolt of lightning that deals 3d12 electricity damage, and the target must attempt a DC 25 Reflex saving throw. If this arrow is shot from a weapon with a shock property rune, the save DC increases to 27, though the attack doesn't benefit from the shock property rune itself.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.
Success The creature takes half damage and isn't affected by the wind.
Failure The creature takes full damage and is buffeted by winds for 1 round, taking a –2 circumstance penalty to ranged attack rolls and a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its fly Speed.
Critical Failure As failure, but the creature takes double damage.

failure.note: 1 round — –2 circumstance penalty to ranged attack rolls, –10-foot circumstance penalty to fly Speed

criticalFailure.note: 1 round — –2 circumstance penalty to ranged attack rolls, –10-foot circumstance penalty to fly Speed

name: Storm Hammer
description: This +1 warhammer crackles with electricity, dealing 1 extra electricity damage on a hit.

actions.name: Thunderclap Charge
actions.description: Effect Until the end of your turn, the storm hammer deals 1d6 extra electricity damage instead of 1. Once you use this activation, you can't use it again for 10 minutes.

name: Striking
description: A striking rune stores destructive magic in the weapon, adding extra weapon damage dice on a hit.

variants.label: Striking
variants.name: Striking
variants.description: This weapon adds one additional weapon damage die.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Striking (Greater)
variants.description: This weapon adds two additional weapon damage dice.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Striking (Major)
variants.description: This weapon adds three additional weapon damage dice.

name: Studded Leather Armor
description: This leather armor is reinforced with metal studs and sometimes small metal plates, providing most of the flexibility of leather armor with more robust protection.

name: Subtle Armor
description: Using fabrics and fashion accessories, you disguise your armor to look like everyday clothing. After applying this adjustment, you can attempt a Stealth check to to hide the nature of your armor. You gain a +1 item bonus to this check. The added weight and care make it more difficult to move around in the armor, increasing the armor's check penalty by 1, its Strength entry value by 2, and its Bulk by 1.

name: Sulfur Bomb
description: A reeking bomb whose acrid fumes choke and sicken. Deals 1d4 acid and 1 acid splash; on a hit the target takes a −1 status penalty to Perception and attack rolls until the end of its next turn, and on a critical hit it is sickened 1.

strikes.name: Sulfur Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 1d4 acid and 1 acid splash; on a hit the target takes a −1 status penalty to Perception and attack rolls until the end of its next turn, and on a critical hit it is sickened 1.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Sulfur Bomb (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1d4 acid, 1 splash; −1 Perception & attacks; crit sickened 1

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Sulfur Bomb (Moderate)
variants.effect: 2d4 acid, 2 splash; −1 Perception & attacks; crit sickened 1

strikes.name: Sulfur Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 2d4 acid and 2 acid splash; on a hit the target takes a −1 status penalty to Perception and attack rolls until the end of its next turn, and on a critical hit it is sickened 1.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Sulfur Bomb (Greater)
variants.effect: 3d4 acid, 3 splash; −1 Perception & attacks; crit sickened 1

strikes.name: Sulfur Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 3d4 acid and 3 acid splash; on a hit the target takes a −1 status penalty to Perception and attack rolls until the end of its next turn, and on a critical hit it is sickened 1.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Sulfur Bomb (Major)
variants.effect: 4d4 acid, 4 splash; −1 Perception & attacks; crit sickened 1

strikes.name: Sulfur Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 4d4 acid and 4 acid splash; on a hit the target takes a −1 status penalty to Perception and attack rolls until the end of its next turn, and on a critical hit it is sickened 1.

name: Sun Dazzler

actions.name: Flash
actions.description: Activate (one action, manipulate); Requirements A glow rod is loaded (3 Interact actions to load); Effect The loaded glow rod flares in a 30-foot cone. Each creature attempts a DC 24 Fortitude save — dazzled 1 round on a success, or blinded for 1 round on a failure (until it Interacts to rub its eyes).

description: This lantern-like device flashes a loaded glow rod into a blinding cone of light.

actions.name: Cast Ventriloquism
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Duration 10 minutes. Whenever you speak or make any other sound vocally, you can make your vocalization seem to originate from somewhere else within 60 feet, and you can change that apparent location freely as you vocalize. Any creature that hears the sound can attempt to disbelieve your illusion.

name: Superbia Tattoo
description: This tattoo features traditional Varisian geometry and is part of an astrological set of symbol tattoos. Having one provides you with a +1 to Diplomacy checks when speaking to native Varisians, and allows you to cast Ventriloquism once per day. (Usage: tattooed on the body.)

name: Swallow-Spike
description: Your armor responds to your desire to break free of a creature grabbing you by growing spikes. Attacks made by armor with the swallow-spike rune apply the multiple attack penalty as if you had made them with another weapon.

reactions.name: Grow Spikes
reactions.trigger: You become grabbed, restrained, or otherwise held immobilized in a creature's grasp, such as by being engulfed or swallowed.
reactions.description: Your armor suddenly grows spikes, attacking the triggering creature. The armor makes a melee attack with an attack modifier of +14 that deals 2d6 piercing damage. If the creature is swallowing or engulfing you, the attack deals an additional 1d6 damage, and damage from this attack can cut you free if it equals or exceeds the Rupture value of the immobilizing ability. This attack gets an item bonus to the attack roll equal to the armor's item bonus to your AC and an item bonus to damage equal to double that amount.

actions.name: Renewed Assault
actions.description: Requirements You're being held immobilized as described in the rune's reaction; Effect Your armor attacks the creature immobilizing you. The armor makes a melee attack against the creature, as described in the rune's reaction.

name: Swirling Sand
description: Swirling sand carries a faint trace of strange compulsions from the helical sand spire near Beachcomber. Adding this catalyst to a Suggestion spell implants a strange compulsion in one target of the spell. The target creature must spin counterclockwise at the end of its turn if it didn't take a move action that turn. This spin is a free action that has the move trait. This effect lasts for 3 rounds on a success, failure, or critical failure against suggestion (even if the target completes its suggestion in fewer rounds); a target that critically succeeds against suggestion is unaffected by the swirling sand.

catalyst.effect: One target must spin counterclockwise at the end of its turn if it didn't move, for 3 rounds.

name: Tallow Bomb
description: A bomb of rendered fat that clings and burns. Deals no direct damage; 1d4 persistent fire and 1 fire splash. On a critical hit a living target is sickened 1 and can’t reduce its sickened value below 1 while the persistent fire lasts.

strikes.name: Tallow Bomb
strikes.description: Deals no direct damage; 1d4 persistent fire and 1 fire splash. On a critical hit a living target is sickened 1 and can’t reduce its sickened value below 1 while the persistent fire lasts.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Tallow Bomb (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1d4 persistent fire, 1 splash; crit sickened 1 (locked)

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Tallow Bomb (Moderate)
variants.effect: 2d4 persistent fire, 2 splash; crit sickened 1 (locked)

strikes.name: Tallow Bomb
strikes.description: Deals no direct damage; 2d4 persistent fire and 2 fire splash. On a critical hit a living target is sickened 1 and can’t reduce its sickened value below 1 while the persistent fire lasts.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Tallow Bomb (Greater)
variants.effect: 3d4 persistent fire, 3 splash; crit sickened 1 (locked)

strikes.name: Tallow Bomb
strikes.description: Deals no direct damage; 3d4 persistent fire and 3 fire splash. On a critical hit a living target is sickened 1 and can’t reduce its sickened value below 1 while the persistent fire lasts.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Tallow Bomb (Major)
variants.effect: 4d4 persistent fire, 4 splash; crit sickened 1 (locked)

strikes.name: Tallow Bomb
strikes.description: Deals no direct damage; 4d4 persistent fire and 4 fire splash. On a critical hit a living target is sickened 1 and can’t reduce its sickened value below 1 while the persistent fire lasts.

onCritConditions.note: living targets; can't reduce sickened below 1 while the persistent fire lasts

name: Targe
description: This small wooden shield is favored by close-quarters fighters. Most targes feature two thin wooden planks bolted together so that the grains are perpendicular to increase strength. It is light enough to wield a small weapon in the same hand that the targe is strapped to. You can Raise a Shield with your targe while carrying an object of light bulk, or while wielding a weapon of light bulk that has the agile trait. If you make an attack with this weapon while the shield is raised, the shield is no longer raised after the attack.

name: The Hollow Star

reactions.name: Aklo Utterance
reactions.trigger: You cast a spell or use an effect that has the linguistic trait.
reactions.description: You speak in Aklo even if you don’t know it. If the effect requires a spell attack roll, you gain a +1 item bonus to that roll; if it allows a saving throw, all saves against it take a −1 item penalty.

description: This unique black-crystal star hums with alien whispers. It sheds bright light in a 60-foot radius, and any creature within that bright light gains a +1 item bonus to Occultism checks. A lawful carrier is enfeebled 2 while carrying it. The first time you touch it, the next time you sleep you suffer disturbing dreams and must succeed at a DC 25 Will save on waking or be stupefied 1. Deros within its bright light are stupefied 1.

name: Theatrical Mutagen
description: An alchemical mutagen that turns you into a consummate performer, demanding constant motion.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Theatrical Mutagen (Lesser)
variants.effect: The item bonus is +1.

consumable.note: +1 Acro/Crafting/Performance, +5 ft Speed, untrained at level; −1 Perception & Will, off-guard if idle (1 min)

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Theatrical Mutagen (Moderate)
variants.effect: The item bonus is +2.

consumable.note: +2 Acro/Crafting/Performance, +5 ft Speed, untrained at level; −1 Perception & Will, off-guard if idle (10 min)

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Theatrical Mutagen (Greater)
variants.effect: The item bonus is +3 and the Speed bonus is +10 feet.

consumable.note: +3 Acro/Crafting/Performance, +10 ft Speed, untrained at level; −1 Per/Will, off-guard unless you act (10 min)

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Theatrical Mutagen (Major)
variants.effect: The item bonus is +4 and the Speed bonus is +10 feet.

consumable.note: +4 Acro/Crafting/Performance, +10 ft Speed, untrained at level; −1 Per/Will, off-guard unless you act (1 hr)

name: Thieves' Toolkit
description: You need a thieves' toolkit to Pick Locks or Disable Devices (of some types) using the Thievery skill. If your thieves' toolkit is broken, you can repair it by replacing the lock picks with replacement picks appropriate to your toolkit; this doesn't require using the Repair action. If you wear your thieves' toolkit, you can draw and replace it as part of the action that uses it.

name: Throwing Shield
usage: applied to a buckler or shield
description: These special straps and weightings fit over a shield or buckler, but not a tower shield. They are designed to let you doff and throw the shield in one swift movement. You can quickly remove the shield by spending a free action as part of the Strike to throw the shield. When thrown in this way, the shield is a martial thrown weapon that deals 1d6 bludgeoning damage and has the thrown 20 feet trait. A shield with the throwing shield attachment can't have any attached weapons, such as shield spikes or a shield boss, and the adjustments to make it more aerodynamic make it slightly less sturdy, reducing its Hardness by 1.

name: Thunderbird Tuft (Greater)
description: This carved chunk of amber contains a single tuft of gray feathers, which spark with electricity to create an odd jittery sensation in anyone holding the amber for long. When used as part of a Shocking Grasp spell, thunderbird tuft funnels electricity back into the spellcaster in a defensive nimbus. For 1 minute, any creature that touches you or that hits you with a melee unarmed attack or non-reach melee weapon attack takes 2d4 electricity damage, or 2d6 electricity damage if the creature is using a metal weapon.

catalyst.effect: For 1 minute, any creature that touches or hits you with a melee attack takes 2d4 electricity (2d6 with a metal weapon).

name: Thunderbird Tuft (Lesser)
description: This carved chunk of amber contains a single tuft of gray feathers, which spark with electricity to create an odd jittery sensation in anyone holding the amber for long. When used as part of a Shocking Grasp spell, thunderbird tuft funnels electricity back into the spellcaster in a defensive nimbus. For 1 round, any creature that touches you or that hits you with a melee unarmed attack or non-reach melee weapon attack takes 1d4 electricity damage, or 1d6 electricity damage if the creature is using a metal weapon, but the nimbus only lasts 1 round, rather than the full minute.

catalyst.effect: For 1 round, any creature that touches or hits you with a melee attack takes 1d4 electricity (1d6 with a metal weapon).

name: Thunderbird Tuft (Major)
description: This carved chunk of amber contains a single tuft of gray feathers, which spark with electricity to create an odd jittery sensation in anyone holding the amber for long. When used as part of a Shocking Grasp spell, thunderbird tuft funnels electricity back into the spellcaster in a defensive nimbus. For 1 minute, any creature that touches you or that hits you with a melee unarmed attack or non-reach melee weapon attack takes 3d4 electricity damage, or 3d6 electricity damage if the creature is using a metal weapon.

catalyst.effect: For 1 minute, any creature that touches or hits you with a melee attack takes 3d4 electricity (3d6 with a metal weapon).

name: Thunderbird Tuft (Moderate)
description: This carved chunk of amber contains a single tuft of gray feathers, which spark with electricity to create an odd jittery sensation in anyone holding the amber for long. When used as part of a Shocking Grasp spell, thunderbird tuft funnels electricity back into the spellcaster in a defensive nimbus. For 1 minute, any creature that touches you or that hits you with a melee unarmed attack or non-reach melee weapon attack takes 1d4 electricity damage, or 1d6 electricity damage if the creature is using a metal weapon.

catalyst.effect: For 1 minute, any creature that touches or hits you with a melee attack takes 1d4 electricity (1d6 with a metal weapon).

name: Toothy Knife
description: Activate (one-action, manipulate). This jagged bit of metal is pitted and worn but wickedly sharp. The DC of the flat check to end persistent bleed damage dealt by a weapon under the effects of a toothy knife is 17 (or 12 with appropriate assistance). This bleeding still typically ends on its own after 1 minute, as normal.

whetstone.reminder: Persistent bleed this weapon deals ends on DC 17 (12 with appropriate assistance).

name: Toothy Knife (Greater)
description: Activate (one-action, manipulate). This jagged bit of metal is pitted and worn but wickedly sharp. The DC of the flat check to end persistent bleed damage dealt by a weapon under the effects of a greater toothy knife is 19 (or 14 with appropriate assistance). This bleeding still typically ends on its own after 1 minute, as normal.

whetstone.reminder: Persistent bleed this weapon deals ends on DC 19 (14 with appropriate assistance).

name: Torch
description: A torch sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius (and dim light to the next 20 feet) for 1 hour. It can be used as an improvised weapon that deals 1d4 bludgeoning damage plus 1 fire damage when lit.

name: Torrent Spellgun
description: This spellgun fires a jet of pressurized water at a foe. Activate the spellgun by aiming it at one creature and making your choice of a spell attack roll or a firearm attack roll against the target's AC. This spellgun has a range increment of 30 feet. The spellgun blasts a torrent of water that deals bludgeoning damage based on the spellgun's type, then disintegrates into sand.

actions.name: Activate a Spellgun
actions.description:
Interact to level the spellgun at one creature, then Strike: make your choice of a spell attack roll or a firearm attack roll against the target's AC. Then the spellgun disintegrates into sand.

Critical Success The target takes double damage and is knocked back 10 feet.
Success The target takes full damage and is knocked back 5 feet.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Torrent Spellgun (Lesser)
variants.rider: knocked back 5 feet (10 on a crit)
variants.effect: 2d6 bludgeoning; target is knocked back 5 feet (10 on a crit)

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Torrent Spellgun (Moderate)
variants.rider: knocked back 5 feet (10 on a crit)
variants.effect: 7d6 bludgeoning; target is knocked back 5 feet (10 on a crit)

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Torrent Spellgun (Greater)
variants.rider: knocked back 5 feet (10 on a crit)
variants.effect: 12d6 bludgeoning; target is knocked back 5 feet (10 on a crit)

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Torrent Spellgun (Major)
variants.rider: knocked back 5 feet (10 on a crit)
variants.effect: 16d6 bludgeoning; target is knocked back 5 feet (10 on a crit)

name: Tower Shield
description: These massive shields can be used to provide cover to nearly the entire body. Due to their size, they are typically made of wood reinforced with metal. You provide cover for allies behind you when you raise a tower shield, and can increase the bonus to AC from +2 to +4 by Taking Cover after Raising the shield.

name: Transmuting Ingot (Adamantine)
description: Activate (one-action, manipulate). A miniature ingot of metal hangs upon a leather cord, with deep weapon grooves on its surface. A weapon it's applied to counts as adamantine for physical damage it deals for 1 minute, depending on its type.

whetstone.reminder: Counts as adamantine for the physical damage it deals.

name: Transmuting Ingot (Cold Iron)
description: Activate (one-action, manipulate). A miniature ingot of metal hangs upon a leather cord, with deep weapon grooves on its surface. A weapon it's applied to counts as cold iron for physical damage it deals for 1 minute, depending on its type.

whetstone.reminder: Counts as cold iron for the physical damage it deals.

name: Transmuting Ingot (Silver)
description: Activate (one-action, manipulate). A miniature ingot of metal hangs upon a leather cord, with deep weapon grooves on its surface. A weapon it's applied to counts as silver for physical damage it deals for 1 minute, depending on its type.

whetstone.reminder: Counts as silver for the physical damage it deals.

name: Treasure
description: A valuable object with no mechanical use — gems, jewelry, art objects, and other miscellany. Reference this base and override the name, Bulk, and value per item. This base entry carries the shared treasure artwork.

name: Tree Sap
description: This waxed pouch contains thick tree sap. When activated, the face of your shield becomes incredibly sticky. For 1 round the shield gains the grapple trait.

variants.label: Tree Sap
variants.name: Tree Sap
variants.effect: The shield gains the grapple trait for 1 round

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Greater Tree Sap
variants.effect: The shield gains the grapple trait for 1 minute.

name: Tremorsensors
description: These small, metallic devices resemble squashed spheres. They each contain a tiny gyroscope that's incredibly sensitive to vibrations in the earth. While typically worn on one's footwear, the device can be affixed to any part of your armor.

actuated.name: Tremorsensors
actuated.description: Effect You stomp a foot, clap your hands, or create some other source of sound, gaining tremorsense as an imprecise sense with a range of 20 feet for the next 10 minutes.

name: Trickster's Mandolin
description: This ever-changing mandolin is as slippery and protean as the trickster who carries it. While playing the mandolin, you gain a +1 item bonus to Deception and Performance checks. You can Activate the mandolin to Cast a Spell from its staff spell list.

actions.name: Change Instrument
actions.description: You alter the mandolin's color, shape, and apparent type — making it look like a fiddle, a hurdy-gurdy, or any other handheld instrument. The change is purely cosmetic and doesn't alter its function.

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Trickster's Mandolin (Greater)
variants.effect: Adds 2nd- and 3rd-rank spells to the staff.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Trickster's Mandolin (Major)
variants.effect: The item bonus while playing increases to +2. Adds 4th- and 5th-rank spells to the staff.

name: Trident
description: A three-pronged spear. It deals 1d8 piercing damage and has the thrown 20 feet trait.

name: Triton's Conch

actions.name: Sound the Conch
actions.description: Activate (one action, auditory, Interact); Effect For 1 minute, you and all allies within 30 feet gain a +2 item bonus to Athletics checks to Swim and can breathe underwater. Allies apply the effect to themselves.

description: Blowing this great spiral shell calls up the sea’s blessing, letting a group swim and breathe water.

name: Truth Potion
description: On drinking this bitter draught, attempt a DC 19 Will save (you can voluntarily fail); the effect lasts 10 minutes. Failure: you must tell the truth when you speak. Critical Failure: as failure, and when questioned you must attempt another DC 19 Will save or answer truthfully if able. Success: no effect. (Social effect — the GM adjudicates.)

name: Twining Chains
description: This set of chains is completely covered with spikes and sharp blades. Not balanced for weapon use, twining chains are instead wrapped around a user's body as a deterrent to attackers-and, for some, a fashion statement. While wearing twining chains, you gain the Thorns reaction. In addition to their dangers, twining chains' weight also increases your armor's Bulk by 1, Strength entry value by 2, and check penalty by 2.

actuated.name: Thorns
actuated.description: Trigger You are hit by an unarmed attack Effect You twist to harm your attacker with your chains' spikes and blades. Make an attack roll against the triggering attacker using your unarmed attack modifier. On a hit, the attacking creature takes 1d6 piercing damage. This damage increases to 2d6 piercing damage if the armor has a +1 potency rune, 3d6 piercing damage if the armor has a +2 potency rune, or 4d6 piercing damage if the armor has a +3 potency rune. However, whether you hit or miss, you take piercing damage equal to the number of d6s of piercing damage you would deal your attacker.

name: Umbrella
description: A collapsible canvas umbrella on a wooden frame.

name: Undertaker's Manifest

actuated.name: Shroud of Shadow
actuated.description: Activate (free action, concentrate); Frequency once per day; Effect When your next action casts a shadow or void spell prepared from this grimoire, a roiling cloud of shadow spills out, creating dim light in a 30-foot emanation centered on you for 3 rounds (it does not apply where light is already darker than dim).

description: This grimoire of funerary rites is bound in grave-cloth. Once per day it shrouds you in shadow as you cast a shadow or void spell.

name: Valorous Coin
description: Activate (one-action, manipulate). Valorous coins are metal disks emblazoned with two crossed swords, with inscriptions exhorting bravery and optimism. The effects of a valorous coin last for 1 hour. During that time, if you're reduced below a quarter of your Hit Points while wielding the affected weapon, it empowers you with determination and resolve to finish the fight. You gain temporary Hit Points equal to your level that last for 1 minute, and you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Strikes and damage rolls with the affected weapon for 1 minute. Once this minute ends, so do all effects and the remaining duration of the valorous coin, and you're Fatigued until you're healed to your maximum Hit Points.

hpTrigger.note: when the minute ends: Fatigued until healed to maximum HP

whetstone.reminder: If you drop below 1/4 HP while wielding it: temp HP = level and +1 circumstance to Strikes and damage for 1 minute, then Fatigued until fully healed.

actions.name: Cast Sure Strike
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Duration until the end of your turn. The tattoo's foresight sharpens your aim: the next time you make an attack roll before the end of your turn, roll it twice and use the better result. The attack ignores circumstance penalties to the attack roll and any flat check required due to the target being concealed or hidden. You are then temporarily immune to Sure Strike for 10 minutes.

name: Vangloris Tattoo
description: This tattoo features traditional Varisian geometry and is associated with divination and insight. Having one provides you with a +1 to Diplomacy checks when speaking to native Varisians, and allows you to cast Sure Strike once per day. (Usage: tattooed on the body.)

name: Vanishing Coin

activation.trigger: You attempt a Stealth check for initiative but haven’t rolled yet.

description: This dull coin is affixed to armor. When you roll Stealth for initiative, you can activate it to cast 2nd-rank invisibility on yourself, lasting until the end of your next turn.

name: Venom Pouch

activation.trigger: You use Shield Block against an unarmed melee Strike or a Strike made by a non-reach melee weapon.

description: This small brown pouch contains the venom of a poisonous creature. When activated, the triggering creature must attempt a Fortitude save against DC 22. On a failed save, the creature takes 2d6 persistent poison damage.

variants.label: Venom Pouch
variants.name: Venom Pouch
variants.effect: DC 22 Fortitude save; 2d6 persistent poison on a failure

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Greater Venom Pouch
variants.effect: The persistent poison increases to 4d6 and the DC increases to 27.

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Major Venom Pouch
variants.effect: The persistent poison increases to 6d6 and the DC increases to 32.

name: Ventriloquist's Ring
description:
This elegant copper ring has miniature images of songbirds engraved around its circumference. You gain a +1 item bonus to Deception checks.

Activate—Throw Voice (two actions, manipulate; once per day): Twisting the ring around your finger lets you magically throw your voice, with the effects of a ventriloquism spell.

name: Verdant Bola
description: This spellgun seems to be a slingshot full of large nuts at the ends of tangles of vines, rather than some kind of firearm. Activate the spellgun by aiming it at one creature and making your choice of a spell attack roll or a firearm attack roll against the target's Reflex DC. This spellgun has a range increment of 20 feet.

actions.name: Activate a Spellgun
actions.description:
