Interact to level the spellgun at one creature, then Strike: make your choice of a spell attack roll or a firearm attack roll against the target's Reflex DC. Then the spellgun melts.

Critical Success The target is restrained until the end of your next turn, or it Escapes using your spell DC for the DC to Escape.
Success As critical success, except the target is grabbed instead of restrained.

name: Vexing Vapor
description: A bomb of disorienting vapor that fogs the mind. Deals 1d6 mental and 1 mental splash; on a hit the target must succeed at a DC 5 flat check before using concentrate actions until the end of its next turn (1 minute on a critical hit).

strikes.name: Vexing Vapor
strikes.description: Deals 1d6 mental and 1 mental splash; on a hit the target must succeed at a DC 5 flat check before using concentrate actions until the end of its next turn (1 minute on a critical hit).

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Vexing Vapor (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1d6 mental, 1 splash; DC 5 flat to Concentrate

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Vexing Vapor (Moderate)
variants.effect: 2d6 mental, 2 splash; DC 5 flat to Concentrate

strikes.name: Vexing Vapor
strikes.description: Deals 2d6 mental and 2 mental splash; on a hit the target must succeed at a DC 5 flat check before using concentrate actions until the end of its next turn (1 minute on a critical hit).

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Vexing Vapor (Greater)
variants.effect: 3d6 mental, 3 splash; DC 5 flat to Concentrate

strikes.name: Vexing Vapor
strikes.description: Deals 3d6 mental and 3 mental splash; on a hit the target must succeed at a DC 5 flat check before using concentrate actions until the end of its next turn (1 minute on a critical hit).

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Vexing Vapor (Major)
variants.effect: 4d6 mental, 4 splash; DC 5 flat to Concentrate

strikes.name: Vexing Vapor
strikes.description: Deals 4d6 mental and 4 mental splash; on a hit the target must succeed at a DC 5 flat check before using concentrate actions until the end of its next turn (1 minute on a critical hit).

name: Vigilant Eye

actuated.name: Cast Web of Eyes
actuated.description: Activate (Cast a Spell); Frequency once per day; Effect The spellheart casts web of eyes.

actions.name: Cast Detect Magic
actions.description: Activate (Cast a Spell), at will; Effect The spellheart casts detect magic.

description: This lidless crystal eye is a spellheart affixed to armor or a weapon. Affixed to armor, you aren’t off-guard to hidden or undetected creatures of 9th level or lower (you can still be flanked). Affixed to a weapon, your flat check to Strike a target is DC 4 if it’s concealed or DC 10 if it’s hidden or undetected. It can Cast detect magic at will and web of eyes once per day.

name: Viking Shield
description: A round shield usually made of layered wood. Generally between 2-3 feet in diameter, the viking shield is light and extremely mobile for the protection that it affords, but sacrifices some durability for those qualities.

actions.name: Unseen Din
actions.description: You engage a piece of the alien technology, producing a deafening sound as you become completely invisible. Because of the noise, you are only Concealed to other creatures unless they are deaf. This effect lasts until the end of your next turn.

name: Vocoder of Invisibility
description: A curious device found in a crashed spaceship in the deserts of Numeria.

actions.name: Cast Cleanse Cuisine
actions.description: Frequency once per day. You transform all food and beverages in the area into delicious fare, changing water into wine or another fine beverage, or enhancing the food's taste to make it a gourmet treat. You can also choose to remove all toxins and contaminations from the food. This spell doesn't prevent future contamination, natural decay, or spoilage, nor does it make the food any more nutritious.

name: Voratalo Tattoo
description: This tattoo features traditional Varisian geometry and is part of an astrological set of symbol tattoos. Having one provides you with a +1 to Diplomacy checks when speaking to native Varisians, and allows you to cast Cleanse Cuisine once per day. (Usage: tattooed on the body.)

name: War Blood Mutagen
description: An alchemical mutagen that binds a weapon to your hand and fuels relentless aggression.

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: War Blood Mutagen (Lesser)
variants.effect: The attack bonus is +1; weapon-removal DC is 25.

consumable.note: +1 attacks (chosen melee weapon, bound to hand); +1 Will vs fear; concentrate DC 5 flat (1 min)

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: War Blood Mutagen (Moderate)
variants.effect: The attack bonus is +2; weapon-removal DC is 30.

consumable.note: +2 attacks (chosen melee weapon, bound to hand); +2 Will vs fear; concentrate DC 5 flat (10 min)

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: War Blood Mutagen (Greater)
variants.effect: The bonus is +3.

consumable.note: +3 attack (bound melee weapon), +3 Will vs fear; DC 30 to remove, DC 5 flat for concentrate (1 hr)

variants.label: Major
variants.name: War Blood Mutagen (Major)
variants.effect: The bonus is +4.

consumable.note: +4 attack (bound melee weapon), +4 Will vs fear; DC 30 to remove, DC 5 flat for concentrate (1 hr)

name: Warhammer
description: This weapon has a wooden shaft ending in a large, heavy metal head. The head of the hammer might be single-sided or double-sided, but it's always capable of delivering powerful bludgeoning blows.

freeActions.name: Warrior's Insight
freeActions.trigger: You make an attack with a weapon in which you're untrained.
freeActions.description: Frequency once per day; Effect You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to the attack roll. If you are an expert in any weapon, you instead gain a +4 circumstance bonus to the attack roll.

name: Warrior's Training Ring
description: This ring is utilitarian in design, indicating its martial use, with a simple, sharp-edged sculpture at the band's center. While wearing this ring, you add your level to your attack rolls with all weapons in which you're untrained.

name: Water Bomb
description: A bomb of pressurized water that douses flames. Deals 1 nonlethal bludgeoning and 1 nonlethal bludgeoning splash; extinguishes non-magical fire and helps end persistent acid or fire damage (DC 10 flat).

strikes.name: Water Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 1 nonlethal bludgeoning and 1 nonlethal bludgeoning splash; extinguishes non-magical fire and helps end persistent acid or fire damage (DC 10 flat).

variants.label: Lesser
variants.name: Water Bomb (Lesser)
variants.effect: 1 nonlethal bludgeoning, 1 splash; douses fire

variants.label: Moderate
variants.name: Water Bomb (Moderate)
variants.effect: 1d4 nonlethal bludgeoning, 2 splash; douses fire

strikes.name: Water Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 1d4 nonlethal bludgeoning and 2 nonlethal bludgeoning splash; extinguishes non-magical fire and helps end persistent acid or fire damage (DC 10 flat).

variants.label: Greater
variants.name: Water Bomb (Greater)
variants.effect: 2d4 nonlethal bludgeoning, 3 splash; douses fire

strikes.name: Water Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 2d4 nonlethal bludgeoning and 3 nonlethal bludgeoning splash; extinguishes non-magical fire and helps end persistent acid or fire damage (DC 10 flat).

variants.label: Major
variants.name: Water Bomb (Major)
variants.effect: 3d4 nonlethal bludgeoning, 4 splash; douses fire

strikes.name: Water Bomb
strikes.description: Deals 3d4 nonlethal bludgeoning and 4 nonlethal bludgeoning splash; extinguishes non-magical fire and helps end persistent acid or fire damage (DC 10 flat).

name: Waterproofing Wax
description: Many books and spellbooks are treated in this wax formula to protect them from the elements, but waterproofing wax's liquid-repellent properties can be further applied to split up a Grease spell into useful smaller sections. When the spell is cast in an area while using this catalyst, the conjured grease fills three 5-foot squares within 30 feet instead of its normal area; these squares don't need to be contiguous.

catalyst.effect: The grease fills three non-contiguous 5-foot squares within 30 feet instead of its normal area.

name: Waterskin
description: When it's full, a waterskin contains roughly 1 day's worth of water for a Small or Medium creature.

name: Wayfinder
description: A compact bronze compass-like device favored by the Pathfinder Society. Activate (single action, concentrate) to make it shed light like a torch. A wayfinder has a socket that can hold a single aeon stone; a stone slotted into a wayfinder still grants its normal benefits and also grants its resonant power. Removing or slotting a stone takes an Interact action.

name: Weapon Harness
description: A suit of armor with this adjustment incorporates short, flexible harnesses meant to connect weapons to each of its vambraces. These harnesses can each be connected to a melee weapon of light Bulk or less. Attaching or removing a weapon takes an Interact action. Someone else can attach or remove a weapon if you're willing to let them or you're unable to act. You must remove a weapon from its mount before you can completely Release or otherwise stow it. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your Reflex DC against attempts to you of a weapon connected to the armor. If the weapon would be knocked from your grasp or you would drop it, the weapon dangles from the bracer by its harness rather than falling to the ground. You can regain control of the weapon in the normal time it takes you to draw it. Attaching a weapon to a weapon harness prevents you from throwing it or using other abilities that would require it to leave your person.

name: Weapon Potency
description: Magic deepens the weapon's lethality, increasing its item bonus to attack rolls. The weapon can be etched with property runes up to the potency's value.

variants.name: Weapon Potency (+1)
variants.description: Increase the weapon's item bonus to attack rolls by 1. The weapon can be etched with one property rune.

variants.name: Weapon Potency (+2)
variants.description: Increase the weapon's item bonus to attack rolls by 2, and the weapon can be etched with two property runes.

variants.name: Weapon Potency (+3)
variants.description: Increase the weapon's item bonus to attack rolls by 3, and the weapon can be etched with three property runes.

name: Weapon Siphon
description: Activate 1 (manipulate) This set of tubing snakes down the striking surface of a weapon to deliver alchemical explosives. A single lesser alchemical bomb can be fitted to the weapon siphon as an Interact action. The bomb must be one that deals energy damage, such as an Acid Flask, Alchemist's Fire, Blasting Stone, Bottled Lightning, or Frost Vial. The next three attacks made with the weapon deal 1d4 damage of the bomb's damage type in addition to the weapon's normal damage. If the second and third attacks aren't all made within 1 minute of the first attack, the bomb's energy is wasted. These attacks never deal splash damage or other special effects of the bomb and aren't modified by any abilities that add to or modify a bomb's effect. Adding a weapon siphon to a weapon throws off its balance, causing the multiple attack penalty with the weapon to be one greater than usual (usually –6 on a second attack and –11 on a third; or –5 and –10 with an agile weapon).

name: Wig of Holding
description: This magical headpiece is created from planar fibers and contains small extradimensional spaces accessible within the hair. It can hold up to four items of Light Bulk; Bulk held within the wig doesn't add to the wig's own Bulk. You can Interact with the wig to put items inside or remove them, and withdrawing an item takes only a single Interact action, like a worn item, rather than two Interact actions like a stowed item.

name: Wind Ocarina
description: A blue finish decorates the ceramic body of a wind ocarina. Activate (Interact): When you play a note, for 1 minute strong gusts sweep into a 15-foot emanation around you. Ammunition from physical ranged attacks can't pass through the area, and larger thrown weapons take a –2 circumstance penalty to attack rolls if their path crosses it. The area is difficult terrain, and gases—including creatures in gaseous form—can't pass through the emanation.

name: Windstep Sheath
description: Activate (one-action, manipulate). Carved of ash wood and decorated with images of clouds and gusting winds, a windstep sheath is a small icon shaped like a weapon sheath or quiver. This whetstone is favored by duelists who like to take their foe by surprise. When you use the Quick Draw feat with a weapon under the effect of a windstep sheath, you can also Step as part of that action, either immediately before or after your Strike.

whetstone.reminder: Quick Draw with this weapon: also Step immediately before or after the Strike.

name: Winged Sandals

actuated.name: Awaken Wings
actuated.description: Activate (two actions, air, concentrate); Frequency once per day; Effect Wings of force sprout from the sandals, granting you a fly Speed of 30 feet for 10 minutes.

description: These winged sandals slow your falls — whenever you fall, they automatically cast gentle landing on you (they can’t do so again for 10 minutes). Once per day you can Awaken Wings to gain a fly Speed of 30 feet for 10 minutes.

name: Wisp Chain
description: This +1 resilient chain shirt is made of small, interlocking currents of wind carefully woven together. While the links of air don't jingle against each other like chain links might, the tiny cyclones nevertheless create a blustering howl akin to standing at the center of a storm. A creature that ends its turn adjacent to you must attempt a DC 23 Fortitude save. On a failure, it becomes deafened until it moves away from you.

actions.name: Slicing Links
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Effect You unbind the currents that form your armor and release them as cutting whorls of air that slice into creatures in a 60-foot cone. Creatures in the area take 6d6 slashing damage with a DC 25 basic Fortitude save. A creature that fails its save is also pushed 10 feet (or 20 feet on a critical failure). You lose the AC bonus of your armor until the end of your turn, when the air currents reform the wisp chain around you.

aura.effect: it becomes deafened until it moves away from you

name: Wolf Fang
description: This wolf canine is bound in a strip of leather and tied to a buckle or strap on a suit of armor. When the fang is affixed to a weapon and you succesfully trip a foe, you can activate it to deal bludgeoning damage equal to your Strength modifier to the target of your Trip.

name: Wondrous Figurine (Jade Serpent)
description: This 1-inch statuette is carved from jade in the shape of a coiled serpent. When activated it transforms into a living creature with the minion trait that understands your language and obeys your commands. If slain in animal form it reverts to a statue and cannot be activated again for 1 week; if destroyed in statue form its magic is lost. (Minion automation arrives with #1099.)

actions.name: Activate Figurine
actions.description: Activate (two actions, command, Interact); Frequency once per day; Effect You place the statue on solid ground and speak its name, transforming it into a Giant Viper (minion trait) for 8 hours or until reduced to 0 HP.

name: Wooden Shield
description: Though they come in a variety of shapes and sizes, the protection offered by wooden shields comes from the stoutness of their materials. While wooden shields are less expensive than steel shields, they break more easily.

name: Wordreaper
description: This +1 sickle's blade is engraved to look like the curving feather of an elaborate quill, with a pen nib at the base of its wooden handle. You can use the handle as an ink pen, and it never runs out of ink.

actions.name: Read By
actions.description: Frequency once per day; Effect You inscribe the words "read by" and your name inside a nonmagical literary work such as a book, scroll, or play, immediately gaining cursory knowledge of the work as though you had skimmed it for 5 minutes.

reactions.name: Dominant Emotion (Pride)
reactions.trigger: You would be adversely affected by an emotion effect
reactions.description: The hammer tries to neutralize the triggering effect with its own emotion, attempting a counteract check. If the check succeeds, you feel a sudden rush of intense pride and the counteracted effect doesn't take hold of you.

staff.name: Xanderghul's Flawless Hammer
staff.description: At the height of the ancient Thassilonian Empire Emperor Xin crafted seven Alara'quin, magical weapons of extraordinary power. Xanderghul's Flawless Hammer was the symbol of office for Xanderghul, the Runelord of Pride.
